programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Marine sergeant Teddy Lemere is killed by a sniper in Afghanistan just after his sniffer dog helps guide a young child out of a minefield. Abby retrieves a tiny shard of the sniper's bullet from Ted's helmet and finds that it was made in America

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen's cover is blown while he is working undercover and he sends a coded text to Hetty, who effectively cuts him off from NCIS as she makes it the team's top priority to discover who found out his real identity and how. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.desc: Part one of two. Meanwhile, Scanlon investigates the shooting of a family. Paranormal drama, starring Patricia Arquette

programme.desc: Tracey looks forward to mothering Garth, but his lack of enthusiasm puts Sharon in a difficult position. Meanwhile, Dorien discovers a lust for learning - and her new psychology lecturer. Comedy, starring Linda Robson and Pauline Quirke

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon seems to be losing Chris and, more importantly, the ownership of the cafe. Meanwhile, Dorien has to choose between love and luxury. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sam's initiative on the anniversary of Amy Tennant's disappearance gives the team a lead when a friend of the family is seen stealing something from the missing child's bedroom. Emma and Diane investigate an abandoned van and unwittingly set loose a flock of frightened sheep, which turn out to be stolen stock on the way to an illegal slaughterhouse

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: After two members of staff collapse in the middle of surgery, Jac and Joseph are trapped in a quarantined operating theatre with a potentially lethal contamination. While Elliot considers evacuating the hospital, Annalese colludes with Michael after learning a formal investigation has begun into Steve Hewitt's death, and Daisha tries to escape Mark's interference in her life

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: After spending the night with Simon, Lara doesn't have any fresh clothes to wear for work the next day, so turns up in one of her new lover's shirts - a fact that doesn't go unnoticed by Tally, who feels totally dejected by the blossoming romance. A man goes berserk after discovering his daughter has been sexually abused by her grandfather, and Jim tracks Nikki down to the abortion clinic

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Emma works out that abductor Shelley Fitzgerald is likely to take Amy Tennant to her childhood haunts in Brighton. Lewis is the first to congratulate her for getting the hunt back on track, but husband Matt continues to belittle everything she does. Neil leads the team in a frantic search on the Sussex coast, where an elated James Tennant finally sets eyes on his daughter for the first time in a year

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy tries to come to terms with Ted's revelations, and Michelle receives a letter that could change her life

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Michelle has made a decision about her future, but how will Pauline and Arthur take it?

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Elizabeth finds out that Sarah is also in Sydney and arranges a meeting, Olivia and Carolyn make preparations for a card party for Sir Richard and George discovers who Anna's real father is

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Tension grows between Alex and Will after she helps two elderly sisters against his orders, discovering something about herself into the bargain. An invitation to join a neighbouring practice forces her to consider her future at the surgery. Meanwhile, Tom persuades Kerri to face everyone at work by asking her to assist a couple in their mission to get fit for a ballroom dancing contest. Medical drama, starring Maggie O'Neill and Gray O'Brien

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan spreads the word about artificial insemination, James is worried about possible legal action against him, and Siegfried gets on the wrong side of a raging bull. Rural drama, starring Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Peggy finds a letter from Joe Maplin to Clive, leading the camp to believe they have a mole in their midst. Classic comedy, starring Su Pollard and David Griffin

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted finds Clive's classic car gathering dust, and comes up with a devious money-making scheme, in which he raffles it as a prize, but he switches it for an old banger before delivering it to the lucky winner. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane and David Griffin

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth makes expensive vacation plans and discovers that voluntary church cleaning is certainly no holiday. Meanwhile, Rose ends up in a compromising position. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Mary Millar

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Alvin tries to persuade Nora Batty to go for a ride in his sidecar, but she's having none of it. When she relates the story of her long-standing feud with Audrey Craig - who had the audacity to laugh at her hat during a wedding many years ago - it becomes clear the broom-brandishing battleaxe is not one to be messed with

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detectives' case against a violent criminal is thrown into jeopardy by the murder of a key witness. Determined to secure a conviction, they immerse themselves in the seedy underworld of a seaside town, and as they press for answers, Havers is brought face to face with her childhood memories. Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team members have to draw on their powers of detection and discretion when a painting in the Queen's private collection is found to be a fake - and all the clues lead to an art forger and political activist who seemingly committed suicide after a fire destroyed his studio. Meanwhile, prospective grandfather Standing has to sell his beloved Triumph Stag to buy his pregnant daughter a pushchair. Anthony Head guest stars alongside Dennis Waterman and Amanda Redman

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Harry vows revenge on Kachimov following the explosion, but the government demands proof of Russian involvement. Lucas is desperate to return to the Grid, but doubts remain about where his loyalty lies. However, when Ros's efforts to blackmail an oil tycoon produce few results, it seems North may be needed after all. Drama, starring Peter Firth, Hermione Norris and Richard Armitage

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Emma works out that abductor Shelley Fitzgerald is likely to take Amy Tennant to her childhood haunts in Brighton. Lewis is the first to congratulate her for getting the hunt back on track, but husband Matt continues to belittle everything she does. Neil leads the team in a frantic search on the Sussex coast, where an elated James Tennant finally sets eyes on his daughter for the first time in a year

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne Boleyn summons Cromwell, anxious to know whether she has an ally in him, and he witnesses her venting her frustrations on her ladies-in-waiting. The king starts to take notice of Cromwell and comes to rely on the lawyer's advice, but then news arrives from York that Cardinal Wolsey has been arrested by Harry Percy, a vengeful act for denying his betrothal to Anne eight years earlier. Historical drama adapted from Hilary Mantel's Booker prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Cromwell manoeuvres a bill through Parliament acknowledging Henry as head of the Church of England, the first step in enabling the king to grant his own divorce from Katherine of Aragon. The Boleyn family also looks to the lawyer to deal with Harry Percy, whose wife claims her own marriage is unlawful on the grounds that her husband had previously made a binding contract of marriage with Anne Boleyn. This is a task Cromwell approaches with relish, remembering how Percy helped bring down Wolsey. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis and Claire Foy

programme.desc: Comfort is forced to confront her demons when she and Fin are called out to an incident at a boatyard, where she gets trapped with a boy who reminds her of Ric, the lad from the train crash - which still continues to haunt her. programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pauline and Michelle are stubborn with each other and Arthur is caught between them. Ted gains a home but loses an old friend

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Set in a close-knit community in London's East End, these are the beginnings of Britain's favourite soap; intertwining relationships, social issues, triumphs, tragedies, loves and losses

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah attends a back-street doctor, but cannot go through with the operation. Carolyn gets offered a job as art critic for one of Sir Richard's Sydney newspapers

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Will treats a nun with a broken arm and in the process discovers she is dying from an incurable illness, a traumatic revelation that brings him closer to estranged wife Kate. Meanwhile, Tom is plagued by the unwanted attentions of a smitten neighbour. Jenny Seagrove guests

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A smallholder hopes a windfall will mean he can realise his dream of expanding into pig-keeping - until James diagnoses a case of swine fever. Christopher Timothy and Peter Davison star

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Sandy worries she has a stalker when she spots someone following her home at night. Lionel's efforts to calm the situation are unsuccessful, so Alistair steps in to help and manages to uncover the mysterious man's identity

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean ponders giving up work and handing the company over to Judith and Sandy - much to Lionel's delight. Starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Richard finds time weighing heavily on his hands, so Hyacinth decides to provide him with a hobby - a move that backfires on her in spectacular fashion. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Billy and Alvin learn that Truly's ex-wife is trying to find him, and waste no time in gleefully tormenting their pal with the news of a strange woman's appearance in town. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: The detective takes on his most challenging case as he searches for Linda's father-in-law, with the only clue to his whereabouts being an incident on board a submarine 25 years ago. As the case takes a devastating turn, Wallander is plunged into the shady world of Cold War-era politics and faces a battle to protect his daughter. Final episode of the crime drama, starring Kenneth Branagh

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team is given the task of re-examining the unsolved case of peace protester Josh Livesey, found dead near a nuclear base in 1984. Other campaigners pleaded innocence at the time, but refused to co-operate with police, blaming the death on Secret Service skulduggery. Detective drama, starring Alun Armstrong, James Bolam, Amanda Redman and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Ben infiltrates an al-Qaeda cell whose members are planning a bombing campaign in London, and accompanies them on what he thinks is a trial run - only to discover at the last minute that it is all too real. Disturbed to hear details of Lucas's flashbacks to his time in the Russian prison, Harry visits his mentor in an attempt to discover how classified information about a top-secret operation has leaked to the enemy

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Just two days away from retirement, June uncovers a swindle involving property developer Brian Stevens (guest star Mike Reid) and a local authority official involved in the sell-off of the Aldbourne estate. The trail leads close to home and, when DAC Georgia Hobbs is seen kissing John Heaton, June is left not knowing who she can trust

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry does little to hide his disappointment when his queen gives birth to a girl, with Anne aware that her power in court rests on producing a male heir. With the Tudor line remaining vulnerable, Cromwell decrees that everyone in public life must take an oath to recognise Henry's supremacy as head of the English Church, as well as the legality of his marriage to Anne Boleyn, but Thomas More refuses to swear allegiance and is committed to the Tower. Mark Rylance, Claire Foy, Damian Lewis and Anton Lesser star

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Emma is forced to rearrange her plans for the festive period at the last minute, leaving Hester and William disappointed they will not get to spend their first Christmas with their grandson. However, it remains to be seen who will help them consume all the extra food and drink they have to hand. Christmas special of the comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.desc: June and Gina become convinced of Heaton's involvement in the property development conspiracy when he takes charge, announcing he will deal with Georgia in his own way. programme.desc: The Herriots give Highland dancing a fling, while Siegfried catches fleas and a stag disappears. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Lynda Bellingham and Robert Hardy

programme.desc: Jean and Lionel's peaceful weekend in the country is disrupted when they find the faithful Mrs Bale is not her usual cheerful self. To add to their woes, Lionel feels unwell - but a visit from a doctor with a strange bedside manner sheds light on their housekeeper's mood

programme.desc: Hyacinth develops a passion for art, Rose acquires a new admirer, and Daddy has a yearning to join the Foreign Legion. Patricia Routledge stars

programme.desc: The theft and subsequent criminal misuse of DC Tomlin's warrant card adds to DI Raine's troubles as she identifies the body of a local drugs dealer and faces an outbreak of gang warfare on her patch - and it's not long before a second corpse is retrieved from the canal. programme.desc: When a toy store owner is the target of a harassment campaign, the team goes undercover at a telephone exchange to nab the culprit. Period crime drama, starring Lauren Lee Smith

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A dying man asks the team to clear the name of his son, a talented junior golfer who committed suicide after being implicated in the murder of a 10-year-old boy. Aiding the investigation is a former chief superintendent who admits she always had doubts about the teenager's guilt, but the committee members of the exclusive golf club where the victim's body was discovered prove reluctant to help the officers with their inquiries. Isla Blair and Cherie Lunghi guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: An al-Qaeda terrorist leader offers to exchange intelligence on a planned bombing campaign in London for the pardoning of two prisoners, but the team faces a difficult choice when it becomes clear that defusing the explosives would endanger Lucas's role as a double agent. Harry learns the identity of the traitor who passed information to the Russians. Drama, starring Peter Firth and Richard Armitage

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: June and Gina become convinced of Heaton's involvement in the property development conspiracy when he takes charge, announcing he will deal with Georgia in his own way. Evictions begin on the Aldbourne estate and Nikki faces a protest which escalates into a riot when fire bombs are thrown. A familiar face turns up at Sun Hill to see June clock off for the last time

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: The Act of Supremacy has declared Henry the supreme head of the church in England, but Holy Roman emperor Charles V and his ambassador Eustache Chapuys refuse to recognise the new title or the monarch's divorce and subsequent marriage. With Anne pregnant and away from court, Henry begins to take more notice of Jane Seymour, whose family enlist Cromwell's help in their dealings with the king - much to Anne's displeasure. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Clare Foy and Mathieu Amalric

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester and William are disappointed to learn Emma and Peter have made arrangements to be elsewhere over the festive period, so decide to treat their new French friends and neighbours to a typically English Christmas. Seasonal special from 1990, starring Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.title: Galw Nain Nain Nain
programme.desc: Warrington nurse Fflur Hughes, originally from Llanystumdwy, goes on three dates with the help of her grandmother, Olwen Hughes

programme.title: Un Bore Mercher
programme.desc: Faith and Cerys find themselves fighting for Osian at the Court of Appeal in London, as Faith meets up with a friend from the past to get information on Rose. Evan continues to keep a watchful eye on Faith's every move, and Steve bonds with Alys

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties up for auction in Farningham, Kent, Great Barr in Birmingham and Crewe in Cheshire, then discuss the owners' plans

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A fraudster feigns innocence when caught scamming a holiday company. Elsewhere, CCTV shows that an employee claiming compensation was actually drunk when he fell

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Workers undertake major road repairs that must be completed before morning rush hour, and one of the North East's biggest bakeries toil through the night baking fresh products

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams o Liverpudlians take on the antiques challenge in Cheshire, with the help of experts Ben Cooper and Richard Madley. As usual they have one hour to buy three items to take to auction, but also have the added pressure of a shopping challenge chosen by host Eric Knowles. On top of that, one of their items must cost at least £75

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen and Rob get an emergency call from Social Services. Meanwhile, Valerie's jealousy starts to get out of hand

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple hoping to find literary inspiration and seaside serenity for their £500,000 budget on the Dorset coast, and also meets two printmakers

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore items found at Walsall Recycling Centre, including a pair of metal gates and a set of mid-century chairs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Medhurst tour the byways and waterways of the Cotswolds as they search for items to sell for a profit at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: With sky high property prices, home ownership seems out of reach to many. The government's shared ownership scheme is supposed to help get people on the property ladder by letting them buy part of their home and rent the rest, but this programme hears from shared owners who say uncapped service charges, building defects and costly lease extensions have left them stuck in homes with escalating costs and huge debts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Silversmith Brenton West takes a look at a brass replica fire engine, while Andrew Gorrie from Lancashire hopes leatherwork expert Suzie Fletcher can save a fragile leather wallet with an extraordinary history. Elsewhere, the experts set to work repairing a handmade radio system and a banana barrow

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete, first taking on the infamous Skills Test and recreating dishes set by Marcus and Monica. The first two chefs try their hand at Marcus's challenge of preparing scallops with a romesco sauce and a marinated tomato salad, before the second pair of competitors prepare and cook lambs' kidneys and serve on toast with a Madeira mustard sauce for Monica. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: DJ Target, Krept and Konan set two new challenges for the artists. First they must create a beat, before shooting a music video

programme.title: Step into the Ring
programme.desc: Documentary following aspiring young wrestlers from Norwich. Pocket tries to overcome her anxiety and get back in the ring, and Marcus sets out to prove that anyone can become a pro if they are determined enough

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the sixth episode, couple Melissa and Daniel from Dartford face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of a one-bedroom flat in Thornton Heath, south London, a piece of land in Ash in Kent and a semi-detached house in Wednesfield, Wolverhampton

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Anti-fraud teams deal with a crash-for-cash duo when an £8 million scam is exposed, and food poisoning leaves one insurer with a claim for eight holiday-makers

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Delivery drivers are out in force for Black Friday, only to be hampered by closed motorways and mechanical breakdowns. The out-of-hours vet tends to a poorly pup and Gateshead market wholesalers work into the early hours

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is joined by two teams led by experts Charles Hanson and David Harper at an antiques fair in Newbury, Berkshire. The presenter also has a chat with Tim Weeks about the collectable toy market

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Rob struggles to connect with Tom and Ella, and Emma decides to take the plunge with Aashiq. A man has to cancel his stag party but dare not admit the real reason to his best man

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from south east London search for an idyllic rural home in Cambridgeshire on a budget of £625,000. Plus, the presenter visits a fenland potato farm

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rummages around in more people's car boots, coming away from the recycling centre with an antique desk, two barley twist chairs and some grooming sets, hoping she and two other upcyclers can make a profit from them

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The search continues through Gloucestershire and Wiltshire, where Tim Medhurst is excited by a Victorian half crown, while Philip Serrell is overjoyed when he secures a chair made for the investiture of the Prince of Wales in 1969

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Shirley and Tina are disappointed that things have not gone to plan and wonder if Kathy knows what Ian has been up to - and more to the point, whether it will help Mick get the Vic back. Kheerat warns Peter that Ian should stop messing with his family and Mick feigns being happy and supportive as Linda works on her application for the Lucy Beale Foundation prize. A fed-up Thompson says he wants to send Phil to prison or he will send Callum somewhere worse

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: After 15 years of fostering, Stephen and Lynn Smedley were looking forward to their well-earned retirement in Carbis Bay, Cornwall. However, the dream dramatically changed when their daughter died, leaving them caring for their three grandsons. The boys, still grieving for their mum's untimely death, are forced to share a single bedroom with a triple bunkbed. There is only one bathroom and Lynn is forced to do the ironing in the shed due to lack of space. Enter Nick Knowles and the team. Their plan is to reconfigure and enlarge their home to accommodate three growing boys - with the help of hundreds of volunteers

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The third lot of quarter-finalists are challenged to create a dish that makes great use of citrus, presented with an array of fruits from kumquats to lemons, as well as other ingredients. They then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Jay Rayner and Tracey MacLeod, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander talks to a pastor and his wife who plan to build their dream eco house in Brompton in Kent, while Martin Roberts meets a first-time developer in Wolverhampton, and Dion Dublin looks at a huge building which already comes with a tenanted flat and a hair salon in Newcastle-under-Lyme, Staffordshire

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A gang of fraudsters' million-pound wine bar insurance racket is uncovered, and insurers receive a bizarre claim after a dog damages an expensive surgical bed

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: A glimpse into the lives of the invisible army of workers driving lorries, making milk deliveries and cleaning streets at night. Plus, the radio DJ who keeps the insomniacs of Newcastle company

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion trawls the programme's archives and picks out some of the best Victorian antiques bought by the reds and blue teams over the years. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Southwell Racecourse with a mysterious Victorian glass object and Charlie Ross finds out just how seriously the Victorians took crime and punishment on a visit to Anglesey

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: The makers of an acne-treatment product, a family card game and a sport support top designed to prevent injury during exercise watch on while their items are consumer-tested. Presented by Lucy Alexander

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley travels to Somerset to help a couple with a £450,000 budget find their very own patch of greenery after 18 years without a garden. Whilst in the county, Ginny visits the Rural Life Museum to join the medieval multimillion-pound refurbishment project

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling expert Jay Blades uses his salvaging expertise at Altrincham Recycling Centre to search for three items that he can clean up and reinvent. Blacksmith Kev Paxton takes on the challenge of a metal window box in need of a complete makeover, while design team Josh Kennard and Oli Milne are challenged with finding a new use for some stepladders. Jay sees the value in some old tiles - but will he be able to turn all three items into money makers? Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Medhurst are in Wales for their last leg, and both set out with bags of cash from their previous successes. Tim splashes out on some fine silver, while Phil risks a hernia by carrying a grandfather clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former swimmer Fran Halsall, ex-Everton footballer Leon Osman, three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby and Great Britain sprinter Desiree Henry join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A desperate Mick needs to talk, so he calls Katy and confides that seeing her has taken him back to his childhood feeling of being lost and confused. Opening up, he confesses how miserable he has become. Meanwhile, Jack is not happy when he realises he has been played

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: Comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games. The host's cross-examination of the celebs over the course of five weeks provides an entertaining watch as the level of competitiveness and camaraderie grows

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Jo Brand hosts the quiz, inviting US comedian Reginald D Hunter and journalist Charlene White to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they take a satirical sideswipe at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: Denise has a hangover, but claims it is migraine, brought on by stress from the forthcoming wedding. Jim feels depressed by the rest of the family's insistence on ignoring his wishes and celebrating his birthday, so it is cut-price cakes, family photos and bubbly all round

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to music superstar Mariah Carey about her eponymous Magical Christmas Special. TV chef and writer Nadiya Hussain reflects on new book Nadiya Bakes, while British astronaut Tim Peake talks about his autobiography Limitless. Pointless co-presenter Richard Osman discusses his debut crime novel The Thursday Murder Club. Singer-songwriter Gary Barlow also enjoys a brief natter before he performs the single Incredible, from his new solo album Music Played by Humans

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens' Drag Race UK tour arrives in London, where for the first time, Crystal is given the go-ahead to perform a risky aerial routine high above the stage. There is no safety rope, no net and no second chances

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: On their first night off from the tour, the queens hit a local drag show. Vinegar finds some action, while others cut out early like Divina - whose worsening flu continues to plague her

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An amateur detective turns the tables on fraudsters running a crash-for-cash scam, and fur flies as a woman claiming her pedigree cat has been stolen is caught out by social media

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore restyles a bed frame found at the recycling centre in Woking, while experts find a creative new use for some metal ladders and reupholster a small armchair

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Volunteers come to the aid of a family trapped in their car on the causeway connecting the Holy Island of Lindisfarne to the mainland, who face being slowly engulfed by the sea. The crew of one of the RNLI's few inland stations in Ireland assists two capsized kayakers just a few days after Christmas, and on Dorset's Jurassic Coast, a fossil hunter is cut off by a rising tide

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques expert Roo Irvine, trader Danny Sebastian and furniture designer Nessa Doran O'Reilly head to West Kilbride to search for bargains

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George receives the news that his wife Laura's killer may soon be out on parole, while Shay and Siouxsie pick up a mysterious stranger in their car

programme.title: The Big Painting Challenge
programme.desc: The amateur artists travel to Blenheim Palace in Oxfordshire, the birthplace and ancestral home of Winston Churchill. Their first challenge sees them arranging a collection of personal items - from a large yellow hat to a skull - which they must capture on canvas using acrylics. This is followed by a quick 30-minute sketch of a giant chess board, ensuring that light and shadow are just as important as perspective and scale. In their last chance to impress before the elimination, the hopefuls paint the facade of the palace, attempting to capture the drama of its stunning architecture, while Una talks to Churchill's granddaughter Emma Soames

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: To survive the bone-chilling cold, deadly blizzards and darkness of the eight months of an Alaskan winter takes courage, cunning and remarkable endurance. This episode tells the stories of how the tough and resourceful animal and human inhabitants of the windswept mountain peaks, icy tundra and snowbound forests face up to the challenges of the season

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt search for items to sell at auction at the Boconnoc estate in the Cornish countryside. Meanwhile, Paul Martin visits the gardens of Caerhays

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David James, Rhys James, Denise Lewis and Isy Suttie go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball gives all the backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing, and Ian Waite gives his thoughts on the couples' training progress

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Countryfile presenters Charlotte Smith and Tom Heap swap agriculture for antiques as they search Hampshire and Buckinghamshire for bargain collectibles, assisted by auctioneers James Braxton and Phil Serrell. Their final destination is an auction in Winchester - but before that, Tom turns woodworker as he learns the ancient craft of 'bodging' in the Chiltern hills, while Charlotte meets the famous Aylesbury duck - common to the area for 300 years and the inspiration behind Jemima Puddle-Duck

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Lyra the Pomsky moves in with the busy Cuthbertson family in Gateshead, but after the dog chews toddler Ellie's toys, her parent call in Louise to help stop the destruction and restore harmony to the house. In Cardiff, Ailsa has to decide whether she is confident enough to let Chinese crested powderpuff Fabio run free across the beach

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Emily, Margaret and Nancy are all in shock after the violent events of the previous day. Hal is devastated and vows vengeance. Elsewhere, Lucy and Elizabeth fight to keep Golden Square within their grasp. Starring Samantha Morton, Lesley Manville and Liv Tyler

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Ellie Taylor from BBC Two's The Mash Report presents stand-up comedy from London's Hammersmith Apollo, inviting rising star Fin Taylor and the provocative Tez Ilyas to the stage

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. With Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented times, focusing on the issues and challenges that continually dominate the headlines

programme.title: Barack Obama Talks to David Olusoga
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga is joined in conversation by former American President Barack Obama to discuss his long-awaited memoir, A Promised Land. He also reflects on the volatile racial divide in the US, his steadfast refusal to abandon American ideals, and revealing details of life as a black First Family in the White House

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: The team focus on the incredible journeys made by wildlife in autumn. Megan McCubbin catches up with rare whales in the Scottish Highlands, Iolo Williams reveals the science behind nocturnal mass migration, Chris Packham and Megan light up the New Forest to visualise secret fungal networks, and Gillian Burke learns how ducks support their own team colours

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The four strongest chefs from the first two heats return for the quarter-final, inventing a dish that makes the most of an eclectic variety of spices. They then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Jimi Famurewa and William Sitwell, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A fraudster feigns innocence when caught scamming a holiday company. Elsewhere, CCTV shows that an employee claiming compensation was actually drunk when he fell

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore items found at Walsall Recycling Centre, including a pair of metal gates and a set of mid-century chairs

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which Simon Reeve travels through Cornwall as the county emerges from lockdown, discovering the impact of Covid-19 on a part of the country highly reliant on tourism. Simon discovers the ongoing impact of the collapse of the tin-mining industry in the 1980s when he visits one of Britain's poorest estates, and meets the entrepreneurs who sell homemade food on the beach who are struggling to make enough money to last the winter

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Catherine Southon, Gary Barton and Micaela Sharp search the Sussex town of Lewes for bargains, hoping a stethoscope, a vintage mobile and some old furniture will turn them a profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Still upset after her confrontation with Katie, Shay avoids the Turner household. Poppy faces bullying on social media before the school ball, and the election for president of the New Republic of Weld heats up. Comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: The Big Painting Challenge
programme.desc: Una Stubbs and Richard Bacon present the art competition from Tate Britain on the banks of the Thames, where the remaining amateurs focus on the human form for their next three challenges. As they arrive, they are greeted by two semi-naked men, setting the tone for the first task in which they make anatomically correct life drawings. This is followed by the seemingly impossible task of sketching a rhythmic gymnast and her flickering ribbon, followed by the final challenge - a flamenco dance captured in chalk and pastel

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Port Lockroy on Goudier Island in the British Antarctic Territory is the site of one of the world's most remote post offices, which has a staff of four and is open to visiting tourists during the Antarctic summer. They share the neighbourhood with more than 3,000 gentoo penguins, and this documentary follows the trials and tribulations of the birds as they try to raise their chicks in these unusual surroundings. Juliet Stevenson narrates

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Clarks see the harsh reality of poaching in Indonesia when they visit tigers that were caught in snares, prompting Giles to concoct an ambitious plan to save the injured big cats in the wild. Back in Australia, Spot and Stripe are days away from their first birthday and will no longer be classed as cubs, while Spot faces an operation that may end with him losing all vision

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Caroline Hawley and Michael Baggott find objects at the University of Nottingham to take off to auction. Caroline comes across a jacket worn by Nottingham-born Olympic ice skater Jayne Torvill, and Michael gets excited by a 17th-century Bible that is hiding a secret. Host Paul Martin travels to north Nottinghamshire to visit the Portland Collection, which is one of the largest aristocratic art collections in Britain

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David James, Rhys James, Denise Lewis and Isy Suttie test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Weekday fanzine devoted to the ballroom dancing show, featuring interviews with the couples and a sneak peek at what they will be wearing on Saturday with designer Vicky Gill

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Powering Britain
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan meets people working at Britain's biggest operational nuclear site at Heysham on the Lancashire coast. Heysham 1 and Heysham 2 power stations make enough electricity to power more than four million homes. Keeley follows engineers as they undertake a huge maintenance programme called an outage. But with Britain in lockdown during the coronavirus pandemic will engineers be able to get one of the reactors back online to provide much-needed energy to the nation?

programme.title: Saving Britain's Pubs with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Coronavirus is spreading and the British public are told to stay away from pubs. Tom and the landlords he has been supporting face a moral dilemma. Do they keep trying to trade or do they close their doors to fight the virus? However, on March 20 the decision is taken out of their hands when the Prime Minister tells all pubs to close until further notice. Tom started this journey to save four pubs from going under, now every pub in Britain, his own included, find themselves in a fight for survival

programme.title: Pandemic - The Flu That Killed 50 Million
programme.desc: Docu-drama about the 1918 Flu pandemic, which killed more than 50 million people. Using dramatic reconstruction and eyewitness testimony from doctors, soldiers, civilians and politicians, this one-off brings to life the onslaught of the disease and the horrors of those who lived through it. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Rhys James, Maisie Adam and Michael Odewale. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Highlights from the satirical news show, presented by Nish Kumar, as a cast of comedy correspondents give their take on what happened in the week the programme first aired in June 2019

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Secrets of the Masons
programme.desc: Bill Paterson narrates a glimpse into the normally closed world of the Freemasons, with cameras capturing activities at a number of Scottish lodges to shed light on the organisation. Prominent members discuss the conspiracy theories that have swirled around, the extent and influence of membership in the past and present and how, despite an overall decline in numbers, they continue to accept new, young candidates for the brotherhood

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: A light-hearted look at the social and cultural changes society went through since the turn of the 21st century, featuring contributions from politicians and celebrities. In the third episode, Angela Scanlon, Kimberley Walsh and Dane Baptiste take a light-hearted look back at the year 2002, from Justin Timberlake's clothing to the Spooks television series

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Gillian reports on the return of giant blue-finned tuna to Cornwall. Plus, gannets take a leap of faith in Yorkshire, Iolo Williams joins leaping salmon in Wales, Michaela Strachan bids farewell to the seals and Chris Packham looks forward to Winterwatch in January

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An exploration of insurance fraud, including dishonest claimants being caught on camera

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rummages around in more people's car boots, coming away from the recycling centre with an antique desk, two barley twist chairs and some grooming sets, hoping she and two other upcyclers can make a profit from them

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. Sue Nunn from Chichester is hoping wood restorer Will Kirk can bring her late husband's fishing tackle box back to its former glory. Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch have a huge task on their hands when they care for a doll with an extraordinary story. Margaret Dyson's beloved doll, Alice, was badly damaged in 1945 when an RAF plane crash landed into her family home, tragically killing her mother

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques valuer Caroline Hawley, trader Tony Wong and furniture designer Nessa Doran O'Reilly head to Skipton to find some bargains. Once the day is done they have to try and flip items - which include some horse kneepads - for a profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George and Fiona confront Big Mac about his plan to change Weld. With Shay going back to school and staying with her father, Katie rents a tiny house-truck, and Poppy's mother comes to visit Woody and Tracey. Comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: The Big Painting Challenge
programme.desc: Una Stubbs and Richard Bacon present the art competition from Liverpool, and for the amateurs' first challenge they are asked to paint their personal interpretation of the city in watercolours. With just a week until the final, two artists make the bold decision to change their styles in a bid to impress the judges. For the quick draw, the hopefuls capture the Royal Liver Building clock tower, followed by the final challenge - a busy painting of the city's skyline from across the River Mersey

programme.title: The Big Painting Challenge
programme.desc: Una Stubbs and Richard Bacon travel to Dartmouth for the final of the art competition. The four amateurs set up their easels at the Royal Naval College and are asked to capture a sense of Britain's seafaring heritage - and with just three challenges left, one contestant takes a big risk to impress the judges. That's followed by a quick sketch of a platoon of naval cadets marching on the parade ground, before the final painting of the competition, in which they have to capture the atmosphere of Dartmouth harbour. It's then up to judges Daphne Todd and Lachlan Goudie to decide who will be the first Big Painting Challenge winner

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A special edition revisiting some of the historic venues from the series and bringing brand new valuations and unexpected auctions. Plus, presenter Paul Martin goes behind the scenes of Bamburgh Castle in Northumberland to see just what it takes to keep the building alive

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former footballer David James, stand-up comedian Rhys James, ex-track and field athlete Denise Lewis and comedian, actress, and writer Isy Suttie test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the week's ultimate winner finally declared

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Analysis of the couples' progress as they prepare for tomorrow night's show. Gethin Jones is at Elstree Studios with the latest news from behind the scenes

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: As Storm Ophelia hits the British Isles, on the south east coast of Ireland the pagers of the Rosslare crew go off, to attend to a small yacht with three people on board that is caught in one of the worst storms in living memory. In Devon, the crew at Appledore race an incoming tide to try to reach a man stuck fast in quicksand, while on the north coast of Cornwall, the crew of St Agnes must battle through huge breaking waves to reach two young boys being swept out to sea in a deadly rip current

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Tranmere Rovers v Brackley Town (Kick-off 7.55pm). Coverage of the opening match in the second round of the competition at Prenton Park, with a potential money-spinning tie up for grabs in round three for the victors. Brackley, from the National League North, start as underdogs against the hosts, and needed a penalty shoot-out to defeat Bishop's Stortford in round one. Tranmere will be grateful for home advantage for the second round in succession, even without supporters allowed into the ground, having edged past Accrington Stanley 2-1 at the start of the month

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1987
programme.desc: Some of 1987's biggest music stars look back on the year, which saw Margaret Thatcher secure a third term as prime minister, while the Channel Tunnel began construction and Britain was battered by the Great Storm. While those events unfolded in the outside world, the TOTP studio provided a haven for those who wanted to escape. Stock, Aitken and Waterman provided the floor-fillers as hip-hop and rap made their mark on the chart alongside the homegrown House genre. Contributors include Terence Trent D'Arby, Curiosity Killed the Cat, Rick Astley, Kim Appleby, Andy Bell, Carol Decker and Belinda Carlisle

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's national and international news stories. With Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: There is celebration throughout the fleet when the Galactica finds the Battlestar Pegasus, a warship long thought destroyed by the Cylons with the rest of the colonial forces. However, tensions soon begin to surface as the new admiral tries to assert her authority and two of Adama's crew are sentenced to death

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part one of two. As the conflict between Galactica and Pegasus threatens to end in all-out war among the fleet, the Battlestars are forced to reunite to combat a new menace, a Cylon 'Resurrection' ship. Sharon struggles to put her attack behind her, while Adama and Roslin discover the fate of Admiral Cain's civilian passengers

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Journalist Josh Baker investigates the story of former Indiana resident Samantha Sally and her family. After arriving in Syria, her husband became an Islamic State sniper and her young son Matthew was forced to take part in an infamous propaganda video. Sally, who is now in prison, describes her life with the terror group and the part she played in buying sex slaves

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam
programme.desc: The development of steam power, from the earliest experiments in the ancient world to modern-day nuclear power stations. Fred travels to Cornwall to investigate the use of early engines to pump water out of the tin mines

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that shows even a tiny shanty by a pond can be a warm place in the dead of winter

programme.title: The Mayflower Pilgrims: Behind the Myth
programme.desc: Drama-documentary exploring the truth behind the story of William Bradford and the Mayflower pilgrims, whose voyage to America in the early 17th century is celebrated in the US every year as Thanksgiving. The programme explores the circumstances that compelled them to leave Europe and the ugly reality behind the famous story of their arrival in the new world

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: An exploration of Australia's underwater ecosystem, one of the natural wonders of the world and the largest living structure on the planet. In the first of three programmes, marine biologist Monty Halls examines the complex form of the coral reef itself and the wildlife that lives on it, from deadly fish-hunting snails and sharks that can walk on land to fighting corals and parrotfish, which cocoon themselves every night

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The director Sofia Coppola discusses the movies that have influenced her career, including To Die For, Safe and Fish Tank. Throughout the programme, she also reflects on her working methods and the influence her father Francis Ford Coppola had on her early viewing habits

programme.title: Fish Tank (2009)
programme.desc: Angry and rebellious teenager Mia lives with her single mother and sister in an Essex council estate. Suspended from school, she searches for an escape from her claustrophobic existence and becomes attracted to her mother's new boyfriend, driving the family even further apart. Drama, starring Katie Jarvis and Michael Fassbender

programme.title: Frank Lloyd Wright: The Man Who Built America
programme.desc: Jonathan Adams embarks on a journey across the US to examine some of the buildings designed by Frank Lloyd Wright, the architect, interior designer and writer whose pioneering creations are thought to have defined the architectural identity of 20th-century America. Throughout his trek, Adams aims to illustrate how Wright's formative years spent in a Welsh Unitarian community in Wisconsin inspired in him a love of nature, and a desire to question and defy convention that would come to define his signature designs

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that shows even a tiny shanty by a pond can be a warm place in the dead of winter

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam
programme.desc: The development of steam power, from the earliest experiments in the ancient world to modern-day nuclear power stations. Fred travels to Cornwall to investigate the use of early engines to pump water out of the tin mines

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: An exploration of Australia's underwater ecosystem, one of the natural wonders of the world and the largest living structure on the planet. In the first of three programmes, marine biologist Monty Halls examines the complex form of the coral reef itself and the wildlife that lives on it, from deadly fish-hunting snails and sharks that can walk on land to fighting corals and parrotfish, which cocoon themselves every night

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam
programme.desc: The revolution in transport brought about by steam power, charting the technology's development from the earliest colliery railways, through the first passenger train services and to the end of an era in the 1960s

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints an old dirt road leading up to a quaint country barn nestled in nature's greenery

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: In an attempt to leave the men free for front-line fighting, Mainwaring and Wilson decide to recruit women into the platoon to take care of the more tedious tasks such as polishing brass. However, the captain loses his heart to an attractive widow, and it is not long before gossip spreads. Comedy first broadcast in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier, with a guest appearance by Carmen Silvera ('Allo 'Allo!)

programme.title: Brief Encounter (1945)
programme.desc: A suburban housewife meets a married doctor by chance in a railway station waiting room. Later, their paths cross many times until the pair become friends and their meetings a fixed arrangement. But as their feelings for each other transform from friendship into passion, both come to realise the affair is doomed to fail. David Lean's romantic drama, with Celia Johnson, Trevor Howard and Cyril Raymond

programme.title: Big Screen Britain
programme.desc: Ben Fogle explores Carnforth Station, the location for David Lean's classic romance Brief Encounter. He meets Margaret Barton, who played Beryl, and retraces the footsteps of Celia Johnson and Trevor Howard to the Lake District, where they shared their passionate kiss

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines techniques and conventions behind classic movie genres. Here he focuses on one of the most popular - romantic comedies, from early offerings such as Bringing Up Baby to more recent hits like The Big Sick and La La Land

programme.title: What We Were Watching
programme.desc: Grace Dent embarks on a joyful journey back through the BBC archives, celebrating the broadcaster's song-and-dance extravaganzas of the 1960s, 1970s and 1980s. A host of stars are featured, such as Cilla Black, Cliff Richard, Lulu, Twiggy and Shirley Bassey, as well as some of the biggest dance troupes of the time, including Pan's People and The Young Generation

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The director Sofia Coppola discusses the movies that have influenced her career, including To Die For, Safe and Fish Tank. Throughout the programme, she also reflects on her working methods and the influence her father Francis Ford Coppola had on her early viewing habits

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints an old dirt road leading up to a quaint country barn nestled in nature's greenery

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam
programme.desc: The revolution in transport brought about by steam power, charting the technology's development from the earliest colliery railways, through the first passenger train services and to the end of an era in the 1960s

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: Historian Dominic Sandbrook follows up his 2012 documentary series The 70s by moving on to the following decade. He begins by examining how powerful new forces of choice and consumerism radically reshaped British life - a new culture of consumer-driven populism that propelled Margaret Thatcher to victory. He takes in everything from the popularity of Delia Smith and affordable fashions on the high street, to the subcultures of Britain's youth and the crisis of identity that rocked and splintered the political left

programme.title: Fern Britton Meets
programme.desc: The singer and actress discusses how her Catholic faith has helped her overcome obstacles in her life, and how she suffered from anxiety at the height of her fame

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on April 12, with performances by artists including Jesus Jones, Technotronic featuring MC, Janet Jackson, Heart, Bizz Nizz, Paula Abdul, the Cure, Madonna and the Quireboys

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on April 19, with performances by artists including Sonia, the Quireboys, Adamski, the Blues Brothers, Alannah Myles, the Adventures of Stevie V, Faith No More, the Family Stand and Madonna

programme.title: Rod Stewart: Reel Stories
programme.desc: The singer talks to Dermot O'Leary about his life. From Abbey Road to the Royal Albert Hall and Motown to Maggie May, Dermot takes Rod back to his childhood cinema to relive moments from a career spanning six decades

programme.title: Gerry Rafferty: Right Down the Line
programme.desc: A profile of the singer-songwriter, who died in January 2011 and is best remembered for solo hits including Baker Street and Get It Right Next Time, and Stuck in the Middle with You by Stealers Wheel. The programme traces his life through his often autobiographical songs and features contributions by his daughter Martha, brother Jim and friends and colleagues including Billy Connolly, John Byrne and Joe Egan. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: The Proclaimers: This Is the Story
programme.desc: Superfan David Tennant meets Craig and Charlie Reid of Scottish band the Proclaimers, as they discuss the 30 years since their debut album, and a career that has taken them from playing in small pubs and clubs around Scotland to become one of the nation's most iconic bands

programme.title: Annie Lennox: BBC One Sessions
programme.desc: The Academy Award-winning singer performs at LSO St Luke's, London, with members of the BBC Concert Orchestra. The session includes songs from the Eurythmics' back catalogue, together with material from her solo career. First shown in 2009

programme.title: Travis with the BBC SSO at the Barrowland
programme.desc: Rock band Travis join forces with the 60-piece BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra at Glasgow's Barrowland Ballroom for a collaboration arranged for BBC Music Day in June 2016. Songs performed in front of the 1,000 audience members include All I Want to Do Is Rock, Sing and Why Does It Always Rain on Me?

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: A performance by the Brit Award-winning artist at the BBC Radio Theatre in London, recorded in 2018. The multiple Brit Award-winning singer and songwriter established herself as one of the UK's most exciting artists in 2012, with the release of her debut album Our Version of Events. The album spent a total of 10 weeks at number one and was the bestselling album of 2012 in the UK. Her follow-up Long Live the Angels came in 2016 and featured the singles Hurts and Breathing Underwater

programme.title: The Proclaimers: This Is the Story
programme.desc: Superfan David Tennant meets Craig and Charlie Reid of Scottish band the Proclaimers, as they discuss the 30 years since their debut album, and a career that has taken them from playing in small pubs and clubs around Scotland to become one of the nation's most iconic bands

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Part one of two. The cast of Jamie Johnson travel the UK in search of the country's most talented, inspiring and dedicated young footballers. This episode features Matthew, a talented footballer from the remote Isle of Barra in Scotland who travels hundreds of miles across land and sea to play football. Having been chosen to join a top academy on the mainland he's preparing to move to Glasgow permanently - but not before playing in the most important annual match on the island.

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jermaine Johnson travels to Glasgow to meet Matthew, a talented footballer from a remote island off the coast of Scotland. Scouted by a top Scottish academy, he is leaving the island life to move to one of the biggest schools in Europe in order to pursue his footballing ambitions - but will he cope with the new challenge?

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Maddie Murchison, who plays Zoe in Jamie Johnson, visits a grassroots club in north London to meet Teddie, a talented footballer. With bigger clubs asking after him, Teddie and his dad have a serious decision to make in the run-up to next season - will they move to a pro academy, or stay and develop his game at grassroots level?

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Lenna Gunning Williams, who plays Jack in Jamie Johnson, travels to Manchester to meet 16-year-old Jess. Having excelled at her previous club, Jess was signed by Manchester City Women's Academy a year ago and after a break-out season, she has already been selected as part of the senior squad. Will she have what it takes to cope with the step up?

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Jermaine Johnson, who plays Wozza in Jamie Johnson, meets up with gifted footballer Ezra. Signed to a top academy at six years old, he regularly returns to the grassroots club in south London where he learnt his skills, in order to keep his competitive edge. Jermaine joins him for the final session before the new season starts, but will he be able to cope with the pressure and keep up with the high standards?

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Louis Dunn is in his home town of Manchester to meet Idris, a young amputee footballer with big dreams. Spotted at school by a disability coach from Manchester City, Idris now plays every week with a community team. Idris has his eye on a more competitive future, and is hoping to make it to the final training session for the England Amputee team before the World Cup in Mexico

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson

He notices some caterpillars on a nearby branch dancing to the music, but when he tries to approach them, they get frightened and curl up. Realising that they aren't afraid of him while he makes music, Love Monster builds them a miniature stage and happily performs for them. programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are going to Westman Islands, Iceland, where the world’s largest colony of Atlantic puffins gathers every year. Lars loves these tough little birds and explains how, although they spend most of their lives flying out to sea and fishing, they return to land each year to lay their eggs. When the baby birds, also called pufflings, eventually hatch, they fly out towards the ocean. But the light from nearby towns often confuses them about where to go, leading local children and adults to form 'puffin patrols' to rescue the pufflings. Lars is excited to help out but is upset when he discovers Glitch, also a fan of puffins, is already well-known on the islands for his skills as a puffin rescuer. Although Glitch offers him a place in his puffin patrol, Lars is determined to outdo Glitch with a patrol of his own - but when he recruits a clumsy Grimbot, things take a turn for the worse...

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are in Australia, exploring the Wolfe Creek Crater, also known as Kandimalal. While Ubercorn explains how the crater was formed by a meteorite, Foz searches it for fragments of the space rock, as objects from space often contain precous metals. But soon he has competition in the form of Glitch, who races Foz to find an object that both of their metal detectors pick up - which turns out to be a rusty spoon! In the end, neither of them manage to find any pieces of meteorite, prompting Glitch to send the Grimbots to drag a huge space rock to Earth to help him! Can the Go Jetters prevent this huge disaster before it's too late?

The boys, still grieving for their mum's untimely death, are forced to share a single bedroom with a triple bunkbed. There is only one bathroom and Lynn is forced to do the ironing in the shed due to lack of space. Enter Nick Knowles and the team. programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former swimmer Fran Halsall, ex-Everton footballer Leon Osman, three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby and Great Britain sprinter Desiree Henry join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A desperate Mick needs to talk, so he calls Katy and confides that seeing her has taken him back to his childhood feeling of being lost and confused. Opening up, he confesses how miserable he has become. Meanwhile, Jack is not happy when he realises he has been played

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: Comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games. The host's cross-examination of the celebs over the course of five weeks provides an entertaining watch as the level of competitiveness and camaraderie grows

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Jo Brand hosts the quiz, inviting US comedian Reginald D Hunter and journalist Charlene White to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they take a satirical sideswipe at the week's events

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Rhod works at the UK's largest open cast mine in Merthyr Tydfil, where he is tested on everything from driving a 25-ton truck to handling. Moving onto Europe's deepest mine, Rhod has to learn to work nine kilometres under the North Sea with a foreman who has no time for his whingeing

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to music superstar Mariah Carey about her eponymous Magical Christmas Special. TV chef and writer Nadiya Hussain reflects on new book Nadiya Bakes, while British astronaut Tim Peake talks about his autobiography Limitless. Pointless co-presenter Richard Osman discusses his debut crime novel The Thursday Murder Club. Singer-songwriter Gary Barlow also enjoys a brief natter before he performs the single Incredible, from his new solo album Music Played by Humans

programme.title: Dark Land: Hunting the Killers
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old schoolgirl Muriel Drinkwater was murdered in 1946, brutally assaulted in the woods near her home in Penllergaer, before being shot. Over the decades, although the culprit has never been caught, several key discoveries have been made to advance the investigation. But due in part to the scientific limitations of the day, such discoveries failed to produce major breakthroughs. Here, the team applies modern-day forensic techniques to the available facts. What investigators uncover edges Muriel's killer closer to being identified and provides answers to at least some of the questions that her family has been asking since the day she died

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens' Drag Race UK tour arrives in London, where for the first time, Crystal is given the go-ahead to perform a risky aerial routine high above the stage. There is no safety rope, no net and no second chances

programme.title: God Shave the Queens

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: With sky high property prices, home ownership seems out of reach to many. The government's shared ownership scheme is supposed to help get people on the property ladder by letting them buy part of their home and rent the rest, but this programme hears from shared owners who say uncapped service charges, building defects and costly lease extensions have left them stuck in homes with escalating costs and huge debts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Silversmith Brenton West takes a look at a brass replica fire engine, while Andrew Gorrie from Lancashire hopes leatherwork expert Suzie Fletcher can save a fragile leather wallet with an extraordinary history. Elsewhere, the experts set to work repairing a handmade radio system and a banana barrow

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete, first taking on the infamous Skills Test and recreating dishes set by Marcus and Monica. The first two chefs try their hand at Marcus's challenge of preparing scallops with a romesco sauce and a marinated tomato salad, before the second pair of competitors prepare and cook lambs' kidneys and serve on toast with a Madeira mustard sauce for Monica. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: DJ Target, Krept and Konan set two new challenges for the artists. First they must create a beat, before shooting a music video

programme.title: Step into the Ring
programme.desc: Documentary following aspiring young wrestlers from Norwich. Pocket tries to overcome her anxiety and get back in the ring, and Marcus sets out to prove that anyone can become a pro if they are determined enough

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the sixth episode, couple Melissa and Daniel from Dartford face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: After 15 years of fostering, Stephen and Lynn Smedley were looking forward to their well-earned retirement in Carbis Bay, Cornwall. However, the dream dramatically changed when their daughter died, leaving them caring for their three grandsons. The boys, still grieving for their mum's untimely death, are forced to share a single bedroom with a triple bunkbed. There is only one bathroom and Lynn is forced to do the ironing in the shed due to lack of space. Enter Nick Knowles and the team. Their plan is to reconfigure and enlarge their home to accommodate three growing boys - with the help of hundreds of volunteers
