programme.desc: Ginny Buckley travels to Somerset to help a couple with a £450,000 budget find their very own patch of greenery after 18 years without a garden. Whilst in the county, Ginny visits the Rural Life Museum to join the medieval multimillion-pound refurbishment project

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling expert Jay Blades uses his salvaging expertise at Altrincham Recycling Centre to search for three items that he can clean up and reinvent. Blacksmith Kev Paxton takes on the challenge of a metal window box in need of a complete makeover, while design team Josh Kennard and Oli Milne are challenged with finding a new use for some stepladders. Jay sees the value in some old tiles - but will he be able to turn all three items into money makers? Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Medhurst are in Wales for their last leg, and both set out with bags of cash from their previous successes. Tim splashes out on some fine silver, while Phil risks a hernia by carrying a grandfather clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former swimmer Fran Halsall, ex-Everton footballer Leon Osman, three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby and Great Britain sprinter Desiree Henry join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A desperate Mick needs to talk, so he calls Katy and confides that seeing her has taken him back to his childhood feeling of being lost and confused. Opening up, he confesses how miserable he has become. Meanwhile, Jack is not happy when he realises he has been played

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: Comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games. The host's cross-examination of the celebs over the course of five weeks provides an entertaining watch as the level of competitiveness and camaraderie grows

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Jo Brand hosts the quiz, inviting US comedian Reginald D Hunter and journalist Charlene White to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they take a satirical sideswipe at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: Denise has a hangover, but claims it is migraine, brought on by stress from the forthcoming wedding. Jim feels depressed by the rest of the family's insistence on ignoring his wishes and celebrating his birthday, so it is cut-price cakes, family photos and bubbly all round

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Tim McGarry, Colin Murphy, Jake O' Kane and Neil Delamere poke fun at the people and events that have been making the news in Northern Ireland, putting their comedic spin on questions posed by audience members joining the show via Zoom

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to music superstar Mariah Carey about her eponymous Magical Christmas Special. TV chef and writer Nadiya Hussain reflects on new book Nadiya Bakes, while British astronaut Tim Peake talks about his autobiography Limitless. Pointless co-presenter Richard Osman discusses his debut crime novel The Thursday Murder Club. Singer-songwriter Gary Barlow also enjoys a brief natter before he performs the single Incredible, from his new solo album Music Played by Humans

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens' Drag Race UK tour arrives in London, where for the first time, Crystal is given the go-ahead to perform a risky aerial routine high above the stage. There is no safety rope, no net and no second chances

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: On their first night off from the tour, the queens hit a local drag show. Vinegar finds some action, while others cut out early like Divina - whose worsening flu continues to plague her

programme.title: 100 Women Extra
programme.desc: Finland has a coalition government led by five women - four of them are in their 30s. Their prime minister, Sanna Marin, was the youngest world leader when she was elected. Finland's coalition government has been praised for their quick and decisive action on the coronavirus pandemic, but a coalition can also mean compromise. The BBC has had world-first exclusive access behind the scenes to explore how the coalition works in practice, how representative it is of Finland's society, and what impact the age and sex of the government's main players has had on its policies and effectiveness.

programme.title: Witness History
programme.desc: For this special, women-focused edition of Witness History, the BBC's Ciru Muriuki in Nairobi brings us five moments from the recent past as told to us by the women who were there. We hear from the daughter of the world's first female prime minister. We also learn about the fight for equal rights for women at the Western Wall in Jerusalem and the campaign for the right to take the contraceptive pill in Japan. Plus, Nasa's female space pioneers and one of the crew members of the Discovery Shuttle mission that put into orbit one of the most remarkable scientific instruments of all time, the Hubble telescope.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Click
programme.desc: To celebrate BBC 100 Women, Click profiles the most inspiring and influential women in technology, from the leaders shaping our future to those finding solutions to the challenges thrown up by the pandemic. The women featured are those driving change, not just for women, but for all parts of society. Hearing their inspirational stories, we learn not only how these women are making an impact, but also the challenges they've faced and the diverse backgrounds they come from. Using in-depth profiles, we provide audiences with a rare and intimate glimpse into the lives of the women who are transforming the world of technology at a unique time in our history. Women who are steering global technology policy, fighting bias in artificial intelligence, using data from sewers to combat Covid-19 and creating fertility solutions. Others have founded companies focusing on mental health, online abuse, women's health, vertical farming and current transport restrictions. With rare-access only Click can

programme.title: The Firing Line
programme.desc: 2020 has been heavily defined by the Covid-19 pandemic, which has led to social disruption around the world and the deepest economic recession since the Great Depression. The year has also seen a record number of entries for the Rory Peck Awards. The awards pay tribute to an international field of freelance journalists around the world who have continued to capture some of the most dramatic and important news and current affairs stories - working in difficult and dangerous circumstances. The Firing Line, presented by Yalda Hakim, features the finalists and the winners of the awards.

programme.desc: Including a live set by Attic Lights, recorded at Glasgow's QMU, and sessions by Broken Records and Tommy Reilly. Camera Obscura drop in to talk about their life in music

programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of dealers set up camp in Hartlepool, where the gloves are off for Henry Nicholls and there is a musical treat from Simon Schneider

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Lance, Sophie, Elaine, Sean, Jake, Bridget, Nesta, Chidimma and Alex are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Roy, Jan, Stephanie and Zaki pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jacob leaves for Portugal, Ellis has a suggestion for Al, and Priya hopes her wishes will become a reality

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne begs Imran to help her with Oliver's case, and when he refuses, she resorts to desperate measures. Johnny confesses the truth to Margaret, and Ray makes Geoff an offer for Number 6

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Great British Escape
programme.desc: In a new six-part series, Paul O'Grady shares the beautiful sights, stories and secrets of his beloved adopted home county, Kent. In a year where many people have chosen to holiday closer to home due to the Covid-19 pandemic, Paul embraces the opportunity to rediscover what the county has to offer. In Dover Harbour, Paul boards a speed boat to blast along the iconic white cliffs, where he has an encounter with a colony of harbour seals living on a disused MOD Navy base. Later, he arrives in Whitstable to meet the West family, who have been sorting, selling and shucking oysters for five generations

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve implores Leanne to put an end to Oliver's suffering and let him die with dignity, Jenny fears for Johnny's future, and Geoff informs Alya that he is selling the house

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: James heads to Philadelphia, where Reading Terminal Market provides all manner of ingredients for his dishes. James then takes a tour of the city's famous murals, sees one of the most impressive car collections in the world and hears about the rise of vegan eating restaurants in the city

programme.title: Griff's Great Australian Adventure
programme.desc: Riding an historic Tiger Moth bi-plane, Griff Rhys Jones arrives in the humid tropics of Darwin. He encounters a crocodile and an iconic news editor, before travelling through the night on a road train, arriving in the 'red centre' of Alice Springs - only to be surprised by the lush green sporting fields and the mysterious Americans playing baseball there. The presenter gets some tips on how to survive in the desert from an Aboriginal elder, before riding into the sunset on a camel - in tribute to the Afghan cameleers for which the legendary Ghan passenger train is named

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak asks Ned to be his best man. Lisa is furious to find out where her wedding ring came from. Andy and Robert break into Outhwaite's home only to receive a big shock

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andy wants to go back to Outhwaite's, but Robert is too scared. Kelly is determined to go ahead with her plan to con Chris. Paddy asks Mandy to live with him

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker manages to arrest one member of a robber gang when a sting operation goes wrong - but ends up being taken hostage by the man's brother. The PC's disappearance is bad news for Blaketon, who's frantic to pull together a winning side for the annual cricket match against rivals Scarsdale. Cricketers Martin Bicknell, Ray Illingworth and Brian Close guest star, alongside Dickie Bird as the umpire

programme.title: Bless This House

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid has problems when Jean pleads for a new freezer

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Little Birchmarch is besieged by traffic until Ida's novel approach makes the Highway's Chief act

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. The twins abscond from Brockhurst College, but Pop manages to bring them back safe and sound - then lends his services to ensure the boarding school does not have to close. Meanwhile, Primrose makes an unusual request concerning affairs of the heart and Charley launches a bid to buy a local brewery. David Jason stars

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family members are surprised to find out that Leslie has three different girlfriends, so Louisa decides to take it upon herself to help him pick one by having each member of the family invite their favourite girl to afternoon tea. Larry injures his leg and Theo decides to use his newly set-up x-ray unit to try and examine it. Elsewhere, Gerry continues to grow his animal collection, Margo takes up a job as Theo's assistant, and Aunt Hermione arrives in Corfu for good

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police search for a boy who went missing after discovering his mother was having an affair. Younger strikes up an unlikely friendship as he fills in for the local lollipop lady, while Walker finds he isn't over Helen during a date with one of Gina's friends, and Carol's fiance Frank gets time off from the Navy to visit her. Sixties-set drama, with Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy worries that Paddy will change his mind about them living together, and tells Biff that Kelly is pregnant with his baby. Ned informs Sarah that Jan is coming home for a visit

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andy hides the cash so Jack and Sarah do not find it, Marlon confronts Andy and Robert about terrorising Outhwaite, and Kelly is angry with Mandy for telling Biff about the baby

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma apologises to Mike for thinking him capable of arson, Tricia is indignant when he questions her, and Maxine is put out when Ashley does not want to see her again

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie is startled to find Chris is sleeping at the garage. Jim prepares for Steve's home visit. Judy tells Gary she thinks they may need help in conceiving a baby

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's former CID colleague Jack Colby left the force under a cloud following a sex scandal involving villagers and officers at Causton police station. Now retrained as a probation officer, Jack is working at a halfway house for reformed criminals, so when he is bludgeoned to death the detective sets out to discover why the past would not leave him alone. With Chris Barrie, Edward Petherbridge, Sue Jenkins, Nigel Harrison and Christopher Fairbank

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: In the final episode of the ninth series, Martin rushes to Ruth's for a medical emergency, and preparations for Morwenna and Al's wedding do not go to plan. Elsewhere, Martin takes his final GMC assessment under the watchful eye of eminent surgeon Bernard Newton, who monitors Martin's skills in assisted surgery. Starring Martin Clunes, Tom Conti, Caroline Catz and Jessica Ransom

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Things take a gruesome turn when Ferabbees Circus comes to town, bringing with it a chain of sinister clown sightings, threatening notes and deathly dangerous circus acts. Barnaby has to face up to his fears in order to solve the case. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Nick Hendrix, Lorraine Ashbourne and Annette Badland

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: After Will and Leonard witness a deadly and deliberate hit-and-run, Geordie traces the car to a pair of decidedly dysfunctional brothers, whose house lies far off the beaten path. The victim's wife, his employee and the two brothers are all damaged characters caught in a web of lies and betrayals - and it's not just secrets hiding within the siblings' grounds. Back at home, Will and Geordie both struggle as unwelcome guests look set to stay - Will's mum Amelia seems to have found a carbon copy of his late father in her new beau. Meanwhile, a new television set lands in the vicarage - just in time for Eurovision. Robson Green, Tom Brittney and Al Weaver star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A former bookie falls under suspicion for the murder of a hospital benefactor, who is found dead in his car. As the detectives investigate the case, it becomes clear their prime suspect had a strong motive, believing the victim was responsible for putting his daughter into a coma. Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

Gerry becomes fascinated by a wall in the back garden that is covered in insects

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Chaos reigns after an unknown sniper shoots a woman as she leaves a meeting of the flower show committee. DS Rachel Dawson's enquiries reveal the gunman may have missed his intended target - and when Blaketon spots an article in the local paper, he realises the bullet might have been aimed at him. Meanwhile, Peggy buys an ice-cream van to take advantage of the hot weather, but makes the mistake of leaving David in charge of the project. Lindsey Coulson guests alongside Derek Fowlds and Clare Wille

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tony is confident his new business will be a success. Ned tells Jan that he needs her back home. Roy tells Kelly he knows she's pregnant and offers to help

programme.desc: Tara is attacked by an unknown assailant while she is in bed. Biff tells Ned that Alex is back and they head to Oakwell Hall to make him pay for Linda's death

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike tells the police about Don's confession. Jim and Andy try to keep the news of Fiona's engagement from Steve. Angie meets Chris and takes an instant fancy to him

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Milton Cross, a feudal estate with a church, farms, houses and acres of land, depend on lord of the manor Edward Milton for their livelihoods. When local woman Sonia Woodley is stabbed to death in the churchyard two years after the death of her husband, bespoke tailor Gerald, Barnaby and Jones investigate, and the village begins to reveal its secrets. Detective drama, guest starring James Wilby, Gwyneth Strong and Karl Davies (formerly Emmerdale's Robert Sugden), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A former bookie falls under suspicion for the murder of a hospital benefactor, who is found dead in his car. As the detectives investigate the case, it becomes clear their prime suspect had a strong motive, believing the victim was responsible for putting his daughter into a coma. Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy drama series starring Martin Clunes as Dr Martin Ellingham, an eminent surgeon forced to retrain as a GP after developing an aversion to blood. Learning a vacancy has arisen in the sleepy Cornish village of Portwenn, Martin promptly applies and gets the job, despite opposition from local schoolteacher Louisa. With Caroline Catz, Stephanie Cole and Celia Imrie

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A comic convention is held in Carver Valley, but the event gets off to a sinister start when the latest creation of a mysterious writer is stolen from the organiser's house and burned. Barnaby takes up the case, which turns murderous when a former supermodel is killed during the event. The only clue is an unusual issue of the missing comics, which seems to reveal the dark secrets of six local people - and provides the police with six likely suspects. Guest starring Bill Bailey and Jemma Redgrave

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Will and Ellie go on their first date with a trip to the flicks. However, a scream from the projection booth cuts the romance short as they discover a brutal murder has taken place within the cinema. Geordie arrives to investigate and soon they've found movie star connections, oddball employees and a link to the Asian subcontinent, as a shadowy figure emerges from Jack Chapman's past and refuses to retreat without causing a stir. Cathy's mother takes the children out for the day, which ends up worrying Cathy far more than it should. Robson Green, Tom Brittney, Lauren Carse and Kacey Ainsworth star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A homicidal rapist escapes from prison and embarks on a deadly game of cat and mouse with Morse, relying on his mastery of disguise and bizarre occult connections to stay one step ahead of the law. Only the madman's psychiatrist seems able to provide a lead as to his whereabouts - and her life is in mortal danger as a result. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Keith Allen, Richard Griffiths and Harriet Walter

programme.desc: Comedy series. When a storm makes him homeless again, Wilfred has to find a new dwelling

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: David Jason stars as Pop Larkin, the country-dwelling bumpkin with a heart of gold for whom everything has to be just 'perfick', with Pam Ferris as Ma Larkin and Catherine Zeta-Jones in her breakthrough role as the couple's voluptuous eldest daughter Mariette. Philip Franks also stars as the tax inspector who finds himself drawn into the family's easy-going world

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Larry and Louisa return to England. While his mother is reunited with her cousins, Larry returns to London, where he falls in with a group of Bohemian artists - including writer Henry Miller. Back in Corfu, Leslie gets offered a job as a policeman, Gerry saves a donkey from an abusive farmer, and Spiro forbids Margo from seeing Zoltan

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield policemen are called to subdue anti-war protestors at a disused airfield, but the event has serious repercussions for Walker after he accidentally hits one of the mob with his truncheon. Younger is gutted to discover Rosie has feelings for new vet Fergus, who is the indirect cause of chaos when a cobra escapes from his car at the garage. Irritated that nobody's taking her snake-catching suggestions seriously, Peggy refuses to help with the hunt and drives off with David - straight into the arms of danger. With Jonathan Kerrigan, Gwen Taylor, Vanessa Hehir and Andrew Langtree

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of day one of the Winter Festival, including the 3.00 Ladbrokes Long Distance Hurdle, and races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.35. With analysis from Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett, Luke Harvey and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy tries to help Sam to read. Biff tells Kim about Steve's deal with Alex. Kelly tells Chris that she is pregnant. Heather and Lyn accuse Eric of swindling Outhwaite

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is fuming with Steve over his involvement with Alex. Chris tells Kelly he will help her get an abortion. Lyn finds Outhwaite unconscious on the floor of one of the barns

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Chris spends the night on Angie's sofa. Steve is convinced Fiona will marry him and not Alan. Mike plans to record Don confessing to starting the fire

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don tells Ashley he was joking about setting fire to the factory. Vera worries that Tricia and Ray are getting serious. Jim tells Andy he must stop feeling sorry for himself

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The medic struggles to dissuade the locals from using his surgery as a drop-in centre and finds himself forced to deliver bad news to a former teacher already bitter at losing his job to Louisa. Adding to his woes, the decision to sack incompetent receptionist Elaine results in the entire village ostracising him. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz and Lucy Punch

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer Parva hosts the revival of a traditional pagan festival, but tragedy strikes when curate Alex Dakin is burned alive inside a straw effigy of a woman. The body count rises as an apparent victim of spontaneous combustion, a budding photographer and a newly arrived schoolteacher are the next to be roasted alive. Keith Barron guest stars

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a young man is found dead and stark naked out on the Fens, Will and Geordie discover ties to a Cambridge research project experimenting with mind-altering drugs. Its arrogant professor is a proponent of the drug LSD in his psychotherapy sessions, but Will and Geordie start wondering whether there is more going on. As the case proceeds, Will's feelings of guilt about his father's death and his conflicted feelings about Ellie start to take their toll. Elsewhere, the Keatings suffer an awkward dinner party and the Chapman feud shows no sign of relenting. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An opera singer is shot by a sniper at an Oxford University ceremony just as she is about to receive an honorary degree. Morse is a big fan of her work and thinks she could not possibly be involved in anything untoward, but his investigations turn up more than a few sordid secrets. Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz

Cricketers Martin Bicknell, Ray Illingworth and Brian Close guest star, alongside Dickie Bird as the umpire

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The couple go to a family wedding, but they don't bargain on Jean's cousin Peggy being there

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid has problems when Jean pleads for a new freezer

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Little Birchmarch is besieged by traffic until Ida's novel approach makes the Highway's Chief act

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. The twins abscond from Brockhurst College, but Pop manages to bring them back safe and sound - then lends his services to ensure the boarding school does not have to close. Meanwhile, Primrose makes an unusual request concerning affairs of the heart and Charley launches a bid to buy a local brewery. David Jason stars

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family members are surprised to find out that Leslie has three different girlfriends, so Louisa decides to take it upon herself to help him pick one by having each member of the family invite their favourite girl to afternoon tea. Larry injures his leg and Theo decides to use his newly set-up x-ray unit to try and examine it. Elsewhere, Gerry continues to grow his animal collection, Margo takes up a job as Theo's assistant, and Aunt Hermione arrives in Corfu for good

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police search for a boy who went missing after discovering his mother was having an affair. Younger strikes up an unlikely friendship as he fills in for the local lollipop lady, while Walker finds he isn't over Helen during a date with one of Gina's friends, and Carol's fiance Frank gets time off from the Navy to visit her. Sixties-set drama, with Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy worries that Paddy will change his mind about them living together, and tells Biff that Kelly is pregnant with his baby. Ned informs Sarah that Jan is coming home for a visit

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andy hides the cash so Jack and Sarah do not find it, Marlon confronts Andy and Robert about terrorising Outhwaite, and Kelly is angry with Mandy for telling Biff about the baby

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma apologises to Mike for thinking him capable of arson, Tricia is indignant when he questions her, and Maxine is put out when Ashley does not want to see her again

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie is startled to find Chris is sleeping at the garage. Jim prepares for Steve's home visit. Judy tells Gary she thinks they may need help in conceiving a baby

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's former CID colleague Jack Colby left the force under a cloud following a sex scandal involving villagers and officers at Causton police station. Now retrained as a probation officer, Jack is working at a halfway house for reformed criminals, so when he is bludgeoned to death the detective sets out to discover why the past would not leave him alone. With Chris Barrie, Edward Petherbridge, Sue Jenkins, Nigel Harrison and Christopher Fairbank

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: In the final episode of the ninth series, Martin rushes to Ruth's for a medical emergency, and preparations for Morwenna and Al's wedding do not go to plan. Elsewhere, Martin takes his final GMC assessment under the watchful eye of eminent surgeon Bernard Newton, who monitors Martin's skills in assisted surgery. Starring Martin Clunes, Tom Conti, Caroline Catz and Jessica Ransom

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Things take a gruesome turn when Ferabbees Circus comes to town, bringing with it a chain of sinister clown sightings, threatening notes and deathly dangerous circus acts. Barnaby has to face up to his fears in order to solve the case. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Nick Hendrix, Lorraine Ashbourne and Annette Badland

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: After Will and Leonard witness a deadly and deliberate hit-and-run, Geordie traces the car to a pair of decidedly dysfunctional brothers, whose house lies far off the beaten path. The victim's wife, his employee and the two brothers are all damaged characters caught in a web of lies and betrayals - and it's not just secrets hiding within the siblings' grounds. Back at home, Will and Geordie both struggle as unwelcome guests look set to stay - Will's mum Amelia seems to have found a carbon copy of his late father in her new beau. Meanwhile, a new television set lands in the vicarage - just in time for Eurovision. Robson Green, Tom Brittney and Al Weaver star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A former bookie falls under suspicion for the murder of a hospital benefactor, who is found dead in his car. As the detectives investigate the case, it becomes clear their prime suspect had a strong motive, believing the victim was responsible for putting his daughter into a coma. Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sally interpret Sid's dreams and Trevor tries to recreate the atmosphere of the previous night

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is not feeling his usual self, so he goes to see the doctor

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: While Angie's career in commercials goes from strength to strength, Ida fears that her son's marriage is growing weaker. Will Ida be able to stop herself interfering?

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. The twins abscond from Brockhurst College, but Pop manages to bring them back safe and sound - then lends his services to ensure the boarding school does not have to close. Meanwhile, Primrose makes an unusual request concerning affairs of the heart and Charley launches a bid to buy a local brewery. David Jason stars

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Leslie decides that he wants to be with Dionisia, and prepares to let Daphne down gently - however, it turns out that she is pregnant with his child, and her father is determined that they must get married as soon as possible. Gerry becomes fascinated by a wall in the back garden that is covered in insects

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Chaos reigns after an unknown sniper shoots a woman as she leaves a meeting of the flower show committee. DS Rachel Dawson's enquiries reveal the gunman may have missed his intended target - and when Blaketon spots an article in the local paper, he realises the bullet might have been aimed at him. Meanwhile, Peggy buys an ice-cream van to take advantage of the hot weather, but makes the mistake of leaving David in charge of the project. Lindsey Coulson guests alongside Derek Fowlds and Clare Wille

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tony is confident his new business will be a success. Ned tells Jan that he needs her back home. Roy tells Kelly he knows she's pregnant and offers to help

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tara is attacked by an unknown assailant while she is in bed. Biff tells Ned that Alex is back and they head to Oakwell Hall to make him pay for Linda's death

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike tells the police about Don's confession. Jim and Andy try to keep the news of Fiona's engagement from Steve. Angie meets Chris and takes an instant fancy to him

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Milton Cross, a feudal estate with a church, farms, houses and acres of land, depend on lord of the manor Edward Milton for their livelihoods. When local woman Sonia Woodley is stabbed to death in the churchyard two years after the death of her husband, bespoke tailor Gerald, Barnaby and Jones investigate, and the village begins to reveal its secrets. Detective drama, guest starring James Wilby, Gwyneth Strong and Karl Davies (formerly Emmerdale's Robert Sugden), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A former bookie falls under suspicion for the murder of a hospital benefactor, who is found dead in his car. As the detectives investigate the case, it becomes clear their prime suspect had a strong motive, believing the victim was responsible for putting his daughter into a coma. Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy drama series starring Martin Clunes as Dr Martin Ellingham, an eminent surgeon forced to retrain as a GP after developing an aversion to blood. Learning a vacancy has arisen in the sleepy Cornish village of Portwenn, Martin promptly applies and gets the job, despite opposition from local schoolteacher Louisa. With Caroline Catz, Stephanie Cole and Celia Imrie

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A comic convention is held in Carver Valley, but the event gets off to a sinister start when the latest creation of a mysterious writer is stolen from the organiser's house and burned. Barnaby takes up the case, which turns murderous when a former supermodel is killed during the event. The only clue is an unusual issue of the missing comics, which seems to reveal the dark secrets of six local people - and provides the police with six likely suspects. Guest starring Bill Bailey and Jemma Redgrave

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Will and Ellie go on their first date with a trip to the flicks. However, a scream from the projection booth cuts the romance short as they discover a brutal murder has taken place within the cinema. Geordie arrives to investigate and soon they've found movie star connections, oddball employees and a link to the Asian subcontinent, as a shadowy figure emerges from Jack Chapman's past and refuses to retreat without causing a stir. Cathy's mother takes the children out for the day, which ends up worrying Cathy far more than it should. Robson Green, Tom Brittney, Lauren Carse and Kacey Ainsworth star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A homicidal rapist escapes from prison and embarks on a deadly game of cat and mouse with Morse, relying on his mastery of disguise and bizarre occult connections to stay one step ahead of the law. Only the madman's psychiatrist seems able to provide a lead as to his whereabouts - and her life is in mortal danger as a result. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Keith Allen, Richard Griffiths and Harriet Walter

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is in for a shock when he finds out who Jean has enlisted to decorate Mike's bedroom

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Comedy series. When a storm makes him homeless again, Wilfred has to find a new dwelling

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: David Jason stars as Pop Larkin, the country-dwelling bumpkin with a heart of gold for whom everything has to be just 'perfick', with Pam Ferris as Ma Larkin and Catherine Zeta-Jones in her breakthrough role as the couple's voluptuous eldest daughter Mariette. Philip Franks also stars as the tax inspector who finds himself drawn into the family's easy-going world

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Larry and Louisa return to England. While his mother is reunited with her cousins, Larry returns to London, where he falls in with a group of Bohemian artists - including writer Henry Miller. Back in Corfu, Leslie gets offered a job as a policeman, Gerry saves a donkey from an abusive farmer, and Spiro forbids Margo from seeing Zoltan

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield policemen are called to subdue anti-war protestors at a disused airfield, but the event has serious repercussions for Walker after he accidentally hits one of the mob with his truncheon. Younger is gutted to discover Rosie has feelings for new vet Fergus, who is the indirect cause of chaos when a cobra escapes from his car at the garage. Irritated that nobody's taking her snake-catching suggestions seriously, Peggy refuses to help with the hunt and drives off with David - straight into the arms of danger. With Jonathan Kerrigan, Gwen Taylor, Vanessa Hehir and Andrew Langtree

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of day one of the Winter Festival, including the 3.00 Ladbrokes Long Distance Hurdle, and races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.35. With analysis from Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett, Luke Harvey and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy tries to help Sam to read. Biff tells Kim about Steve's deal with Alex. Kelly tells Chris that she is pregnant. Heather and Lyn accuse Eric of swindling Outhwaite

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is fuming with Steve over his involvement with Alex. Chris tells Kelly he will help her get an abortion. Lyn finds Outhwaite unconscious on the floor of one of the barns

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Chris spends the night on Angie's sofa. Steve is convinced Fiona will marry him and not Alan. Mike plans to record Don confessing to starting the fire

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don tells Ashley he was joking about setting fire to the factory. Vera worries that Tricia and Ray are getting serious. Jim tells Andy he must stop feeling sorry for himself

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The medic struggles to dissuade the locals from using his surgery as a drop-in centre and finds himself forced to deliver bad news to a former teacher already bitter at losing his job to Louisa. Adding to his woes, the decision to sack incompetent receptionist Elaine results in the entire village ostracising him. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz and Lucy Punch

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer Parva hosts the revival of a traditional pagan festival, but tragedy strikes when curate Alex Dakin is burned alive inside a straw effigy of a woman. The body count rises as an apparent victim of spontaneous combustion, a budding photographer and a newly arrived schoolteacher are the next to be roasted alive. Keith Barron guest stars

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a young man is found dead and stark naked out on the Fens, Will and Geordie discover ties to a Cambridge research project experimenting with mind-altering drugs. Its arrogant professor is a proponent of the drug LSD in his psychotherapy sessions, but Will and Geordie start wondering whether there is more going on. As the case proceeds, Will's feelings of guilt about his father's death and his conflicted feelings about Ellie start to take their toll. Elsewhere, the Keatings suffer an awkward dinner party and the Chapman feud shows no sign of relenting. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An opera singer is shot by a sniper at an Oxford University ceremony just as she is about to receive an honorary degree. Morse is a big fan of her work and thinks she could not possibly be involved in anything untoward, but his investigations turn up more than a few sordid secrets. Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: TV cameras descend on the wards to make a documentary featuring Dr Thorpe, but mayhem, chaos and disorder rear their ugly heads as the patients vie for attention. Hospital comedy, starring James Bolam, Peter Bowles and Richard Wilson

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar rescues a Russian damsel in distress and agrees to help her fend off art thieves eager to get their hands on her da Vinci masterpieces. Cult adventure series, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: The outlaws enter a mysterious village where their every wish is granted - unaware they are being duped by the evil Gulnar, who is intent on reviving a bloodthirsty pagan deity. Jason Connery stars

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Jim poses as a photographer to investigate a terrorist group's activities. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry helps his friend Des by delivering a car, but it is a getaway vehicle and his fingerprints are found in it. When Terry is arrested and sent to court, Arthur has to act as a character witness, leaving his prospects of acquittal looking decidedly gloomy. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie is captured and held hostage in a vicarage by four German terrorists - and as time runs out, he finds himself forced to make a deal with them before anyone gets hurt. Gabrielle Drake guest stars, with Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A youth is arrested after an elderly money-lender is brutally assaulted and robbed. His mother asks Regan, an old friend, to intervene. Though it is later proved he got a legitimate loan and is innocent of theft, the youngster finds himself being blackmailed after falling foul of some very dangerous individuals. Karl Howman guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Nightclub owner Paul Verrier is tricked into believing that the Saint is trying to kill him, so contacts various people to murder Templer. One of his nefarious contacts is unknown to him visually, so when the Saint eventually pays a call Verrier mistakes him for his hired assassin and inadvertently hires the Saint to kill himself. Roger Moore stars

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Exasperated at the Sheriff's record in office, King John visits Sherwood to personally oversee the destruction of Robin and his outlaw band - a task he entrusts to the ruthless Roger de Carnac. Phil Davis and Matt Frewer guest star

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Jim goes undercover to infiltrate a drugs gang, but the operation runs into trouble when his partner tries to help out. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur and Terry are hired by two farmers to transport a prize bull, little suspecting they are actually being tricked into stealing it. When their error of judgement become apparent, the duo try to redeem themselves by working out a way to get the animal back. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are asked to investigate an incident dating back to 1953 after a death-bed confession reveals the key witness in a major corruption trial did not commit suicide as was believed, but was murdered by the police officers assigned to protect her. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan gets caught in the crossfire between the family of a recently deceased gangland leader and the members of the late man's crime syndicate, regarding the division of the hoodlum's wealth. The hitherto innocent son manages to play-off his parent's ex-cohorts against one another with increasing dexterity and appears only too keen to pick up the duplicitous traits of his late father. Hywel Bennett guest stars, with John Thaw

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which five friends from London with a shared love of music and dance tackle questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge. They then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: The Border Force pulls over a Ghanaian businessman declaring £114,000 in cash, there is love in the air at the unlikely location of T5 arrivals when a blind date takes place and it is the end of an era on the airfield for chief trainer Simon

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Terminal 5 receives a very special visitor, leaving buggy driver Carlo star-struck. British Airways have a new robot on the airfield and Border Force release the hounds in search of dirty money

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole rides through the south of France, where he finds some of the twistiest roads imaginable. Back home, he meets the designers behind Aston Martin's first ever bike and tells the story of the Suzuki 750, the bike nicknamed 'the Kettle'

programme.title: Coogan's Bluff (1968)
programme.desc: An Arizona sheriff is sent to New York to bring an imprisoned murderer back to stand trial. However, when the killer escapes, the lawman employs his own methods to recapture him, antagonising the city's by-the-book police force in the process. Don Siegel's crime drama, starring Clint Eastwood, Lee J Cobb, Don Stroud and Susan Clark

programme.desc: The death of a teenager alerts Quincy to the activities of a sinister figure known only as Uncle Harry, who preys on lonely, frightened runaways newly arrived in Los Angeles

Hywel Bennett guest stars, with John Thaw

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Counterfeiters lead Simon a merry dance from London to Switzerland via Paris, and the involvement of a bogus countess only complicates matters further. Roger Moore stars, with Kate O'Mara and Philip Madoc

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: The sheriff captures Much the miller's son and threatens to hang him, and an ageing outlaw kidnap's the tyrant's nephew. Drama, starring Jason Coney

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Spikings finds his life in danger when he investigates the kidnapping of a police informant's son _ and uncovers corruption at the heart of SI-10. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Crooked travel agent Monty Wiseman persuades Arthur to agree to pick up a coffin at Heathrow airport and store it in the lock-up. However when a coroner's autopsy is ordered, Monty stashes it in a disgruntled Terry's bedroom for safekeeping leading to suspicions that there is more to the corpse than meets the eye. Suzi Quatro and Derek Fowlds guest star, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is approached by a woman desperate to prove her imprisoned father is innocent of murder. As he proceeds, the case causes him to become plagued by doubt and believe he sent an innocent man to jail for the murder of his former partner. Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson star

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan encounters a former nemesis at a party, who drunkenly admits involvement in an earlier robbery in which he was let go due to lack of evidence. He disapproves of his daughter's villainous boyfriend, and Regan suspects he is being exploited to help break the romantic union. However, an even darker plan is being hatched, with the daughter an innocent pawn in a robbery. Kenny Lynch and Derek Francis guest star, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five university students from Coventry answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: On the Road
programme.desc: Tiff Needell buckles up for some high-voltage motorsport as he gets to grips with a fully electric and sideways rallycross machine in Austria. Meanwhile, the team prepare for battle in a full track test of a pair of sports SUVs from Alfa Romeo and Mercedes, before Sid North heads to France to check out a new Peugeot and its battery-powered sibling. Paul gets the keys to a Pagani Zonda Cinque, one of the most expensive and exclusive cars on the planet, for a fast blast through the Italian countryside.

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston narrates the documentary featuring another selection of real-life incidents captured by people on their personal cameras. Highlights include a man who nearly gets taken out by an unsecured load on a van, road rage incidents and a motorist taking impromptu lessons in aquaplaning after a terrifying incident on a wet motorway

programme.title: The Bourne Ultimatum (2007)
programme.desc: Amnesiac rogue assassin Jason Bourne's long-buried memories are reawakened by a British journalist researching the secret organisation that was responsible for transforming him into a killing machine. Meanwhile, a ruthless CIA director becomes convinced Bourne is still a security risk, and plans to dispose of him once and for all. Third instalment of the spy thriller series, directed by Paul Greengrass and starring Matt Damon, Joan Allen, Julia Stiles, David Strathairn, Paddy Considine, Scott Glenn and Albert Finney

programme.desc: Templar rescues a Russian damsel in distress and agrees to help her fend off art thieves eager to get their hands on her da Vinci masterpieces. Cult adventure series, starring Roger Moore

programme.desc: The outlaws enter a mysterious village where their every wish is granted - unaware they are being duped by the evil Gulnar, who is intent on reviving a bloodthirsty pagan deity. Jason Connery stars

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Jim poses as a photographer to investigate a terrorist group's activities. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry helps his friend Des by delivering a car, but it is a getaway vehicle and his fingerprints are found in it. When Terry is arrested and sent to court, Arthur has to act as a character witness, leaving his prospects of acquittal looking decidedly gloomy. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie is captured and held hostage in a vicarage by four German terrorists - and as time runs out, he finds himself forced to make a deal with them before anyone gets hurt. Gabrielle Drake guest stars, with Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A youth is arrested after an elderly money-lender is brutally assaulted and robbed. His mother asks Regan, an old friend, to intervene. Though it is later proved he got a legitimate loan and is innocent of theft, the youngster finds himself being blackmailed after falling foul of some very dangerous individuals. Karl Howman guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Nightclub owner Paul Verrier is tricked into believing that the Saint is trying to kill him, so contacts various people to murder Templer. One of his nefarious contacts is unknown to him visually, so when the Saint eventually pays a call Verrier mistakes him for his hired assassin and inadvertently hires the Saint to kill himself. Roger Moore stars

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Exasperated at the Sheriff's record in office, King John visits Sherwood to personally oversee the destruction of Robin and his outlaw band - a task he entrusts to the ruthless Roger de Carnac. Phil Davis and Matt Frewer guest star

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Jim goes undercover to infiltrate a drugs gang, but the operation runs into trouble when his partner tries to help out. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur and Terry are hired by two farmers to transport a prize bull, little suspecting they are actually being tricked into stealing it. When their error of judgement become apparent, the duo try to redeem themselves by working out a way to get the animal back. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are asked to investigate an incident dating back to 1953 after a death-bed confession reveals the key witness in a major corruption trial did not commit suicide as was believed, but was murdered by the police officers assigned to protect her. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan gets caught in the crossfire between the family of a recently deceased gangland leader and the members of the late man's crime syndicate, regarding the division of the hoodlum's wealth. The hitherto innocent son manages to play-off his parent's ex-cohorts against one another with increasing dexterity and appears only too keen to pick up the duplicitous traits of his late father. Hywel Bennett guest stars, with John Thaw

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which five friends from London with a shared love of music and dance tackle questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge. They then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: The Border Force pulls over a Ghanaian businessman declaring £114,000 in cash, there is love in the air at the unlikely location of T5 arrivals when a blind date takes place and it is the end of an era on the airfield for chief trainer Simon

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Terminal 5 receives a very special visitor, leaving buggy driver Carlo star-struck. British Airways have a new robot on the airfield and Border Force release the hounds in search of dirty money

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole rides through the south of France, where he finds some of the twistiest roads imaginable. Back home, he meets the designers behind Aston Martin's first ever bike and tells the story of the Suzuki 750, the bike nicknamed 'the Kettle'

programme.desc: An Arizona sheriff is sent to New York to bring an imprisoned murderer back to stand trial. Don Siegel's crime drama, starring Clint Eastwood, Lee J Cobb, Don Stroud and Susan Clark

programme.desc: Animal welfare officer Matt Brown receives a call about a family of kittens hiding in the loft space above a shop. However, the rescue does not quite go to plan, leaving one little kitten in a sticky situation. Inspector Anthony Pulfer goes to extreme lengths to save a stray tomcat from an inaccessible courtyard in a busy building site, and Jo visits RSPCA Putney, where she meets a kitten with a crushed paw and helps test a feral ginger cat for a potentially life-changing virus

programme.title: Secrets of Your Supermarket Food
programme.desc: Sian Williams and Stefan Gates lift the lid on the food that goes into the annual festive feast. Stefan visits a turkey farm to investigate the reason for the price difference for a basic white turkey, at around £20, and a Norfolk Black, which can cost £75. Sian discovers how the bitter taste is being bred out of sprouts and there's a report on mince pies, revealing how the most expensive may not be the best tasting. Plus, why do people fall asleep after eating Christmas dinner?

programme.title: The Fall of Anne Boleyn: Execution
programme.desc: Historian Tracy Borman reaches May 19, 1536, the day Anne is set to be executed. She discovers astonishing details of the day - of how Anne, despite being found guilty of adultery, incest and treason, still lived in hope that the King would intervene and save her. The penalty for treason was either burning at the stake or being hung, drawn and quartered. Yet King Henry insisted on the French method of beheading by sword. For this he called over an expert swordsman from France - but as Tracy discovers, there is something intriguing about the timeframe

programme.title: Critical Condition

programme.title: Sunny Bunnies
programme.desc: Shiny is feeling sad, and none of her friends' efforts to cheer her up work - until something unexpected happens

programme.title: Jeremy Vine
programme.desc: The broadcaster and guests discuss the issues of the day, with co-host Storm Huntley joining him for phone-ins and reading out viewers' correspondence

programme.title: Christmas with a Prince: Becoming Royal (2019)
programme.desc: The heir to a European throne whisks his girlfriend, a busy American doctor, off on a much-needed holiday to his own country - where he impulsively asks her to marry him. She happily accepts, unaware that a jealous princess does not approve, and is determined to drive them apart. Festive romantic drama sequel, starring Kaitlyn Leeb and Nick Hounslow

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Dipi decides to end things with Pierce, but not before they have one last fling. Nicolette debates whether to tell Chloe about Pierce before they go on a romantic dinner

programme.title: Christmas Wedding Runaway (2019)
programme.desc: A woman runs away from her wedding after suffering doubts that she really loves her fiance. She tries to sort out her feelings while trapped by a blizzard in a lodge with her grandmother and ex-boyfriend. Romantic drama, starring Sara Mitich and Harmon Walsh

programme.title: Christmas in Evergreen: Tidings of Joy (2019)
programme.desc: Skeptical writer Kate arrives in Evergreen to get the scoop on the town's famed passion for Christmas. The New Yorker soon team up with local man Ben on a hunt for a 50-year-old time capsule, which leads to a remarkable discovery. As Katie and Ben get caught up in the community's past and present, they both begin to envision new possibilities for the future. Romantic drama, starring Maggie Lawson and Paul Greene

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Dipi decides to end things with Pierce, but not before they have one last fling. Nicolette debates whether to tell Chloe about Pierce before they go on a romantic dinner

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The gang throws a going-away party for Rachel, but she prefers to say her goodbyes individually - and one of her friends is left feeling hurt at being overlooked. Meanwhile, Monica supervises the packing for a new life in the suburbs with Chandler, while also keeping a close eye on the mother of their future adopted child

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Jon Bentley visits a theme park to find out which instant camera is the best value for money now the format has recently seen a resurgence. Georgie Barrat goes behind the scenes to find out how consumer tech and VR headsets are being used to bring the real world to life. The G Team answers the calls of a couple who want tech to bring them closer together, while there's another money saving tech tip from Harry Wallop. Presented by Craig Charles

programme.title: Walking Britain's Lost Railways
programme.desc: Rob Bell discovers the now-abandoned lines that unlocked the wild coastline of north Devon. He begins by following the dramatic Barnstaple and Ilfracombe Railway, which once built, proved an instant success, eventually carrying the glamorous Atlantic Coast Express service, direct from London Waterloo. He crosses to the Lynton and Barnstaple Railway, which traverses the wild terrain of Exmoor up to the cliff-top village of Lynton. Rob follows the adventurous, narrow-gauge route, discovering the extraordinary tale of the line's construction and the very short section that has been fully restored

programme.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (1966)
programme.desc: A laconic gunslinger, a ruthless hired killer and a conniving outlaw hunt for a cache of loot during the chaos of the American Civil War. However, each is privy to only part of the information necessary to find it, setting in motion a series of deadly betrayals. The third film in Sergio Leone's Spaghetti Western trilogy, starring Clint Eastwood as the Man with No Name, with Lee Van Cleef and Eli Wallach. Featuring Ennio Morricone's iconic soundtrack

programme.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (1966)
programme.desc: A laconic gunslinger, a ruthless hired killer and a conniving outlaw hunt for a cache of loot during the chaos of the American Civil War. However, each is privy to only part of the information necessary to find it, setting in motion a series of deadly betrayals. The third film in Sergio Leone's Spaghetti Western trilogy, starring Clint Eastwood as the Man with No Name, with Lee Van Cleef and Eli Wallach. Featuring Ennio Morricone's iconic soundtrack

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered and Gibbs and McGee are called in when she is discovered to be carrying ID bearing the name Sgt Patricia Moreno, a US marine known to have died several years earlier. The victim is discovered to have come to America from Afghanistan and a necklace reveals a fingerprint that leads the team to a known assassin. Guest starring Lolita Davidovich

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate when naval cryptanalyst Andrew Wells is shot dead in a park outside Baltimore. At the scene, Tony spots a man who was one of his informants from 15 years earlier when he was a police officer, but who went into hiding after a bid to bring down the Russian mobsters he worked for went wrong. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team works with Abigail Borin of the Coast Guard Investigative Service after a highly suspicious explosion and fire on board an oil rig kills a US marine corps reservist. Abby detects malware in the platform's computer systems, while Ducky finds traces on the victim's body that lead to a lawyer who works for the oil company. Back in the office, a prankster is bringing some light relief and although the likeliest suspect is Tony, he denies any involvement. Crime drama, guest starring Diane Neal

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice helps a widower sell his 200-year-old cottage so he can pack up and start a new life in Scotland. The property is basically sound, but has an air of bachelor neglect made worse by the owner's pet Labrador, which acts like it owns the place

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: The Million Pound Note (1954)
programme.desc: An eccentric multimillionaire bets his equally rich brother that if a man is known to be wealthy, he can obtain everything he needs on credit. To put this theory to the test, they give a £1million note to a penniless seaman, but instruct him never to spend it. Comedy adapted from a story by Mark Twain, starring Gregory Peck, Jane Griffiths, Joyce Grenfell and Wilfrid Hyde White

programme.title: Pursued (1947)
programme.desc: A brooding cowboy yearns to find peace, but his life seems to follow a tragic path because of the actions and hatred of a mysterious one-armed stranger and a forgotten incident from his childhood. Western, starring Robert Mitchum, Teresa Wright, Dean Jagger, Judith Anderson, Alan Hale and John Rodney

programme.title: The Day Will Dawn (1942)
programme.desc: A foreign correspondent working in occupied Norway is assigned to an RAF detail helping freedom fighters to sabotage an important German U-boat installation. He enlists the help of a local sea captain's daughter, but the plan goes wrong and he ends up in enemy hands, facing death by firing squad. Second World War adventure, with Hugh Williams, Deborah Kerr and Ralph Richardson

programme.title: Beneath the Planet of the Apes (1969)
programme.desc: A second astronaut arrives on the strange simian-dominated world searching for his predecessor. As he journeys into the infamous forbidden zone, he discovers an underground cult of psychic mutants. Meanwhile, a military coup has taken place among the ape society, leading to an inevitable battle. Sci-fi sequel, with James Franciscus, Charlton Heston and Kim Hunter

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee II (1988)
programme.desc: The rugged Australian adventurer sets out to save his reporter girlfriend from New York drug dealers. Having rescued her from their clutches, he realises they will soon be coming after him - so he decides to even the odds by luring them to the Outback for a final showdown. Comedy adventure sequel, starring Paul Hogan, Linda Kozlowski, John Meillon and Charles S Dutton

programme.title: Office Christmas Party (2016)
programme.desc: When a CEO threatens drastic action to improve the financial performance of the Chicago branch of her company, the affected manager - who is also her brother - throws an epic festive party to impress a client and close a potential sale which would save jobs. Comedy, starring Jason Bateman and Jennifer Aniston

programme.title: The Heat (2013)
programme.desc: An uptight, ambitious FBI agent is sent to Boston to bring a notorious drug kingpin to justice. She ends up forced to team up with a rebellious, foul-mouthed local police detective who has a very different attitude to law enforcement but shares her inability to work with others. Action comedy, starring Sandra Bullock and Melissa McCarthy

programme.title: I, Frankenstein (2014)
programme.desc: The infamous scientist's monster roams the Earth 200 years after burying his creator, and is drawn into an ancient supernatural conflict. Two factions of magical beings are at war, and the key to ending the conflict lies in tapping the secret of the creature's immortality. Fantasy adventure, starring Aaron Eckhart, Bill Nighy and Miranda Otto

programme.title: Gun Fury (1953)
programme.desc: A wealthy rancher and his fiancee take the stagecoach to California, where they intend to start a new life in the wake of the Civil War. However, during the journey an ex-Confederate outlaw and his gang of thugs attack the coach and kidnap the bride-to-be. Distraught and wounded, the rancher sets out to rescue her and punish her abductors. Western, with Rock Hudson, Donna Reed, Lee Marvin and Phil Carey

programme.title: The Way to the Stars (1945)
programme.desc: A naive young pilot is assigned to a British bomber base during the Second World War, and is involved in the opening skirmishes of the Battle of Britain. He encounters the first wave of American recruits to the Allies, and falls for a local girl - but when his squadron leader is killed leaving a wife and child behind, he questions whether he can afford to marry. Drama, starring John Mills and Michael Redgrave

programme.title: A Man Alone (1955)
programme.desc: A gunman stumbles across the bodies of passengers murdered in a stagecoach robbery. Riding to the nearest town to report the crime, he finds himself accused of the killings and faces a battle to prove his innocence. Western, directed by and starring Ray Milland, with Raymond Burr, Lee Van Cleef, Mary Murphy and Ward Bond

programme.title: Carry On Spying (1964)
programme.desc: A secret formula is stolen by a sinister organisation masterminded by the evil Dr Crow. The British government plans to assign its top agents to deal with the crisis, but owing to staff shortages, has to settle for a bungling spy and his three hapless trainees. Comedy, starring Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Cribbins, Jim Dale, Eric Barker and Victor Maddern

programme.title: X-Men: Days of Future Past (2014)
programme.desc: In a future ravaged by war, the superhero team makes a desperate last stand against a force of deadly robots. Their only hope is to send Wolverine back in time to the 1970s, to join forces with an earlier version of the X-Men and change the events that started the conflict. Superhero adventure sequel uniting the casts of the original movie and prequel First Class, starring Hugh Jackman, Jennifer Lawrence, James McAvoy and Michael Fassbender

programme.title: Taken 3 (2014)
programme.desc: In the final part of the blockbusting big-screen trilogy, ex-CIA agent Bryan Mills' efforts to finally live a quiet life are shattered by the death of his ex-wife, Lenore. Just when things seemingly can't get any worse, he is framed for her murder and must fight to clear his name. Taken 2 director Olivier Megaton returns for this action thriller sequel, starring Liam Neeson, Forest Whitaker, Maggie Grace and Dougray Scott

programme.title: Double Jeopardy (1999)
programme.desc: A man fakes his own death and frames his wife for murder, but after six years in prison she is released. Since the woman has already served her time, she has nothing to lose by really killing him - but her mission of vengeance is complicated by a zealous parole officer determined to stop her. Thriller, starring Ashley Judd, Tommy Lee Jones, Bruce Greenwood and Annabeth Gish

programme.title: Sisters (2015)
programme.desc: Two estranged sisters are reunited when their parents decide to sell the house they grew up in. They return to the family home to clear out their childhood bedrooms, but with their parents away, seize the chance to relive their teenage years and throw one last wild house party. Comedy, starring Amy Poehler, Tina Fey and Dianne Wiest

programme.title: Sea of Sand (1958)
programme.desc: Allied troops fighting in North Africa in 1943 are redeployed as saboteurs behind enemy lines to blow up Rommel's desert fuel dumps. A clash of personalities between officers initially causes problems, but the men overcome their differences to work together. Second World War adventure, starring Richard Attenborough, John Gregson, Michael Craig and Vincent Ball

programme.title: Flame of the Barbary Coast (1945)
programme.desc: A cattle rancher falls for a San Francisco saloon-bar singer, but she proves to be in cahoots with a ruthless card sharp who wins all his money. Not to be outdone, he sets out to hone his card-playing skills for a rematch, unaware the city is poised on the brink of disaster. Romantic drama, starring John Wayne, Ann Dvorak and Joseph Schildkraut

programme.title: Decision at Sundown (1957)
programme.desc: A gunslinger rides into a backwoods town intent on finding his dead wife's former lover and exacting revenge for her death. The man he blames now has total control of the town and has the sheriff at his beck and call. Undeterred, the drifter offers his enemy an ultimatum - he has until sundown to leave the town or face retribution for his crimes. Western, with Randolph Scott, John Carroll and Andrew Duggan

programme.title: White Christmas (1954)
programme.desc: A pair of former soldiers start a new career as nightclub entertainers. They try to boost the popularity of their old commanding officer's struggling winter resort in Vermont by staging a series of extravagant shows, with a little help from their former army buddies and a talented singing sister act. Musical, starring Bing Crosby, Danny Kaye, Vera-Ellen, Dean Jagger, Sig Rumann, Mary Wickes, Anne Whitfield and Rosemary Clooney, featuring seasonal songs by Irving Berlin

programme.title: Knight and Day (2010)
programme.desc: A woman has a chance encounter with a rogue secret agent, who has been framed as a traitor and is on the run from his own superiors. Realising she has been mistaken for his accomplice and is in danger for knowing too much, the fugitive kidnaps her and drags her on a dangerous journey around the world, as he sets out to clear his name and protect a vitally important invention. Comedy adventure, with Tom Cruise, Cameron Diaz and Viola Davis

programme.title: Die Hard 4.0 (2007)
programme.desc: Tough New York cop John McClane is sent to escort a computer hacker to Washington for questioning. During this routine operation he stumbles on a criminal mastermind's plan to launch a technological terrorist attack on the US, and is forced to enlist the hacker's aid in foiling the evil scheme. Action thriller sequel, with Bruce Willis, Timothy Olyphant, Justin Long and Maggie Q

programme.title: Birth of the Dragon (2016)
programme.desc: An aspiring martial artist trains under a young Bruce Lee in 1960s San Francisco, and is drawn into a showdown between his mentor and renowned Shaolin master Wong Jack Man. Martial arts biopic, starring Philip Ng, Billy Magnussen and Yu Xia

programme.title: The Counsellor (2013)
programme.desc: A lawyer who earns a living representing drug dealers makes a fateful decision to take an active role in their criminal activities. He quickly finds himself out of his depth in a criminal underworld he barely understands, where his actions bring deadly consequences. Ridley Scott's crime thriller, starring Michael Fassbender, Penelope Cruz, Cameron Diaz, Javier Bardem and Brad Pitt

programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Hollyoaks
