programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Sabrina and her family become addicted to pancakes. Hilda suffers a dire punishment for failing to fill out immigration forms permitting her to enter the mortal world _ banishment back to the bleak Other Realm. Melissa Joan Hart stars

programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Sabrina is sent a demonic doll at Halloween, while Hilda and Zelda find themselves trapped in an insane asylum where a mad doctor tries to exchange their brains with those of two chickens

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Ted is torn between his architectural aspirations and his relationship with Zoey when he is called in front of a meeting to determine the future of the Arcadian - and his decision becomes even more complicated when he discovers Barney's job could be at risk. Comedy, starring Josh Radnor and Neil Patrick Harris

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Ted begins to have doubts over his ability to complete the Arcadian project - and Barney and Robin are horrified to realise he is considering getting back together with Zoey. Marshall secures an interview for his dream job, but is forced to fight off the effects of food poisoning. Comedy, starring Josh Radnor, with Chi McBride (Pushing Daisies) and Dave Foley (Kids in the Hall)

programme.title: Speechless
programme.desc: JJ's love-match with Izzy becomes a cage match with Kenneth. Maya and Jimmy dismiss Valentine's Day as a holiday for gullible people until they spot a way to profit from it. Unable to find a date to a pre-paid romantic night out, Ray takes Dylan along instead. Minnie Driver, John Ross Bowie and Micah Fowler star

programme.title: Speechless
programme.desc: When Maya insists it's time to meet Izzy, a reluctant JJ arranges a game night for his family and new girlfriend. Ray tries to help, but his attention to detail throws a spanner in JJ's evening. Dylan and Kenneth let their appetites get the better of them

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon uses his holidays to help Amy in her biology lab, but moans about the menial jobs he is given - until she asks him to dissect a brain. Penny grows curious about Leonard's attitude to marriage when Bernadette asks Howard to sign a pre-nuptial agreement

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard, Raj and Howard devise a contest to decide whether Sheldon or Kripke should get Professor Rothman's vacant corner office. Meanwhile, Penny receives the gift of a painting from Amy to celebrate their friendship, but discreetly tries to dispose of it with help from Bernadette

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly reignites a long-standing feud after she invites Murray's unreliable brother to visit at Thanksgiving. Meanwhile, Adam upsets the balance of power between him and Barry after he wins a ball game between them for the first time. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica's new job at the department store is put in jeopardy when Beverly takes advantage of her daughter's employee discount too frequently. Meanwhile, during a trip to the horse racing track, Pops bends the truth to convince Barry that his bad luck has changed for the better. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: A new captain at the Nine-Nine attempts to ingratiate herself with Jake and the squad by inviting them over to her house for a dinner party

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Adrian Pimento shows up unexpectedly to seek Jake and Charles' help after claiming someone is trying to kill him. The rest of the squad endures a Workplace Conflict seminar

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang goes to a bar and runs into Penny's old boyfriend Zack, while Bernadette is left to spend the evening with Sheldon when she cannot go wine tasting with the others. Elsewhere, things do not go to plan when Raj introduces his new girlfriend, Claire. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon discovers a surprise screening of The Avengers is scheduled at a local cinema, complete with a Q&A session with the film's director, Joss Whedon. However, the event just happens to clash with time he'd planned to spend with Amy, so he decides to pay Stuart to be his subtitute for the afternoon while he and the other boys immerse themselves in Marvel superheroics. Meanwhile, Leonard's mother Beverly arrives in town for an extended visit, and Penny takes the opportunity to bond with her new mother-in-law - and to make up for not inviting her to the wedding. Comedy, guest starring Christine Baranski

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: JD starts going out with Danni again, but his night of passion with Elliot keeps preying on his mind. Perry is horrified to realise he has a crush on someone he hates, and Dr Kelso crosses swords with the Janitor over the emergency exit. Guest starring Tara Reid (American Pie)

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: Staff at the Sacred Heart wait with bated breath to see what happens when JD tells Elliot how he feels about her. Meanwhile, Carla panics about the wedding cake and a hypochondriac begs for a full body scan. Comedy, guest starring Tara Reid (American Pie), with Zach Braff, Donald Faison and Sarah Chalke

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel opts to ground Lennox when she discovers she is failing chemistry and tells her to study. However, Mel's father, Senator Russell Burke, shows up and unexpectedly undermines her authority. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel sees Joe having a secret meeting with a girl named Tessa in the basement, and discovers they are on the search for her brother-in-law. Meanwhile, Mel and the nanny begin to suspect Ryder is being bullied at school when he keeps losing his belongings, but only make things worse by trying to get his stuff back. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The entire squad is on high alert when a massive blackout hits Brooklyn. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg as a detective with a light-hearted approach to crime

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Detective Jake Peralta and his eclectic group of colleagues at Brooklyn's 99th Precinct are used to having a commanding officer who doesn't make them work too hard or follow the rules too closely. That all changes when tightly wound Captain Ray Holt takes over, determined to make the dysfunctional squad into the best in the borough. Pilot episode of the American comedy, starring Andy Samberg and Andre Braugher

programme.title: Charmed
programme.desc: Harry and Mel try to crack the Book of Elders. Elsewhere, Macy finds the good and bad parts of her demon side difficult to deal with

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon goes to great lengths to convince Amy that they should have a baby together after they successfully complete a scientific experiment using their DNA samples. Raj is too embarrassed to tell his friends what the new woman in his life does for a living

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon cannot contain his jealousy when he hears that his university colleague Bert has won a prestigious fellowship with an impressive grant. Elsewhere, Howard finds an old remote-controlled toy he made in the past, but the rest of the group do not seemed to be too impressed by it. Stephen Hawking guest stars

programme.desc: Another set of apparently meaningless tasks are handed to five comedians by Taskmaster Greg Davies and his genial companion Alex Horne. Jessica Knappett piles shoes on some butcher's scales, James Acaster tries the emergency alarm on a lift, and Rhod Gilbert smears mustard in his eyes

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Santiago investigate a difficult 'he said, she said' case. Captain Holt becomes suspicious that a cunning escape from jail has taken place after discovering that his lifelong criminal arch-nemesis has died in a prison transport accident

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal and his mother Linda, Denise van Outen and her boyfriend Eddie Boxshall, Chris Eubank Sr and Chris Eubank Jr, Stanley Johnson and Georgia 'Toff' Toffolo, Oti Mabuse and her sisters Motsie and Phemelo, Emily Atack and Kate Robbins, Gyles Brandreth and Sheila Hancock, and Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv critique the week's TV. Shows including including American Pie, The Graham Norton Show, Love Island, Natural World - Walrus: Two Tonne Tusker, Killing Eve, Blind Date, Jeremy Wade's Dark Waters, and The Family Brain Game come under their gaze. First shown in June 2019

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during March 2020, turning their attention to shows including Dragons' Den, Crufts and Antiques Roadshow. ITV drama Liar, Netflix's The Stranger and Channel 4's new dating show Five Guys a Week come under scrutiny, while news coverage of Harry and Meghan and the coronavirus are also appraised

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny endure an awkward night at the cinema, both insisting the outing is not a date. Meanwhile, a terrified Sheldon enlists his friends' help when a bird flies into his apartment, but his behaviour soon leaves them exasperated. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Kaley Cuoco and Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Comic-book store owner Stuart asks Amy out on a date, forcing a jealous Sheldon to consider the future of his relationship with her. Meanwhile, the scientists become obsessed with an unusual new card game, and Penny organises a round of Twister. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons and Mayim Bialik

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on This Morning, Earth's Magical Kingdom, and Ant and Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway. They also cat their eyes over The Mandalorian, The Repair Shop and The Nest. Plus, reflections on the coronavirus, including Prime Minister Boris Johnson's statement. First shown in March 2020

programme.title: Angel

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon refuses to get his hair cut as his regular barber is in a coma - a solitary change that causes chaos in the rest of his well-ordered life. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly complains that Pops is spending too much time with her at home, so Murray takes him to work at the furniture shop. programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Competitive juices are flowing as Jake and the squad compete in the second Jimmy Jab Games - a series of eccentric races and contests held in the precinct building

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and Amy set to work on an uncrackable case, Holt adjusts to a new beat and Hitchcock looks for the love of his life

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Conflict arises when Leonard's newly divorced parents and Sheldon's devoutly religious mother are all in town at the same time. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Scrubs

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: The day of Turk's wedding to Carla dawns, but a patient stops him getting to the church on time. Meanwhile, JD tries to make things up with Elliot by reconciling her with Sean. Comedy, starring Donald Faison and Zach Braff

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel invites a TV reporter to do a story on her and the family to help improve her public image. However, when the journalist discovers she has a male nanny working for her, she decides to change the focus of the piece. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: Charmed

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: Taskmaster

programme.title: The Inbetweeners

After a wild time with the boys ordering items off the internet and raiding the fridge, he wakes up to an unpleasant shock. Comedy, starring Simon Bird and Joe Thomas

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will decides to organise one last adventure for his friends before they all go their separate ways, so the gang heads off for a camping trip in the countryside. While Will draws on all his scout training, Jay, Neil and Simon are not prepared for the great outdoors

However, with two people doing the picking, will they ever be able to agree on who to date?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Salem's determination to get his paws on a means of travelling to outer space sets off a bizarre chain of events which sees Sabrina and himself miniaturised inside a toy spaceship which flies up Libby's nose. Melissa Joan Hart stars

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Marshall is outraged at his boss's negotiation techniques with a powerful company and decides to deal with the matter himself, while Ted takes his architecture class on a trip. Comedy, starring Jason Segel and Josh Radnor

programme.title: Speechless

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: JD enjoys his last week as a resident before finally becoming a fully qualified doctor, while quirky new staff member Dr Molly Clock (Heather Graham) makes a big impression on the wards. Comedy, with Zach Braff

programme.desc: Mel needs to prove that all of her employees are American citizens, but runs into problems because of Joe's divorce hearing. Meanwhile, Lennox decides to go to a school dance with one of her female friends. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel is pleased when Lennox begins dating the mayor's son, believing he will be a good influence on her. Meanwhile, Ryder spends time with an elderly lady when he takes part in his school's community service programme. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake meets an attractive medical examiner at a crime scene, but annoys his colleagues when his attempts to get together with her cause a hold-up with an autopsy report. Meanwhile, Amy discovers Jeffords has some hidden talents when the police sketch artist goes off sick

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The cops of the 99th Precinct become the latest victim of the Vulture, a detective from Major Crimes with a habit of taking over investigations just as they are about to be solved. programme.title: Charmed

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After spending the holidays apart, the gang get together to hear about Sheldon's and Amy's trip to his mother's, Leonard and Penny recall their Christmas tree adventure, and Howard and Bernadette open up about their first days as parents

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: A look back at a classic episode of the soap from 2007. Calvin worries about his brother Sonny when he becomes involved with Warren. John Paul and Craig share a drunken first kiss. Meanwhile, Max has severed all ties with Clare, and Becca has a new cellmate

programme.desc: This seventh series reaches the halfway stage and, with the possible exception of Phil Wang, all five competitors still have high hopes of winning Greg Davies's massive gold-plated noggin. programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey (2012)
programme.desc: Unassuming hobbit Bilbo Baggins is pulled out of his comfortable life when Gandalf the wizard volunteers him for a dangerous quest. He joins a party of dwarves who are on a mission to reclaim their lost homeland, which has been taken over by a dragon. When the group journeys into the Misty Mountains, Bilbo becomes separated from the dwarves and falls into a cave, where he finds himself confronted by Gollum and forced to play a riddle game. It is set in Middle-earth 60 years before the events of The Lord of the Rings and is the first part of Peter Jackson's fantasy adventure trilogy based on JRR Tolkien's novel, starring Martin Freeman, Ian McKellen, Richard Armitage, James Nesbitt, Ken Stott, Cate Blanchett, Ian Holm and Christopher Lee

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin lines up five properties for Michael and his wife Jane from Sussex, who are looking for a holiday home within easy reach of the coast in Spain's Almeria province. They have a budget of £90,000 and dream of finding a traditional property with modern features that the whole family can enjoy

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel the length of Britain turning unwanted junk into profit for their owners. In this episode they're on the south coast, where Henry gets to work an aircraft fuel tank while Simon gets obsessed with an iconic '60s speedboat

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien repurposes an old butcher's block into a coffee table, while Henry Cole restores a pair of go-karts to race against restorer Guy

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Margreeth start to research their new business idea, but can they find the suppliers to help them? Stephanie's big event - her singles night - arrives, while Sophie and James put on a walking tour near their chateau

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton visits West Lothian, where Laura and David are building a traditional Scottish cottage between Linlithgow and the Firth of Forth. These two engineers think they have a blueprint for self-build bliss, which involves Dave tackling nearly everything himself. However, first the weather and then a bundle of joy threaten to undermine their ambitious schedule, pushing Dave to the edge

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former pop singer Danny Karne welcomes guests to the Pavilion, his fashion and rock 'n' roll-themed hotel in central London, and the sports car parked outside wows his visitors. But the petrol-head's decision to take his fellow hoteliers to a motoring museum goes down badly when one of them reveals he has no passion for cars

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: John Luce and his partner Pat Sutton invite their fellow hoteliers to the Fox and Duck, a country inn in Therfield, Hertfordshire. The local golf club provides the guests with adequate entertainment, and they appear to bond as they share friendly banter and a spot of singing at the end of the night. However, the following morning brings with it a shower disaster

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave and Debbie Lang, who run the New Inn in Newbridge-on-Wye, Powys, play host. Dave is a keen pig breeder and owns the on-site butcher's shop as well as running a pub, so he serves some of his award-winning pork products for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Kerstin and Robert Sharpe, the owners of Westleigh Farm in Somerset, play host, and despite the initial cobwebs found by some of the guests, they are soon won over by the size of the showers, the quality of the milk and the generous helpings for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Young meerkat parents Huskie and Beagle have a new baby, Junior, but keepers have to step in when he falls gravely ill. Meanwhile, red panda Nima, who lost her partner last year, meets a new mate, Koda, but the pair do not get off to the best start, and in the penguin pool, a new male called Flounder arrives

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge joins Elaine and David as they look around an 1850s chateau with 25 rooms, telling them what to look out for when buying such a vast property. The programme also catches up with Tim and Krys from the first series, who renovated rooms for a boutique B&B in the Dordogne, but have been struggling to juggle parenthood with running the business

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Chris Moller meets Steve Wilson and Wendy Grell, who want to get out of the city and live the quiet life. They've demolished their old family holiday home and are building a permanent abode at a small, isolated Northland bay, three hours' drive from Auckland. Their vision is to construct an architectural showpiece, with angled walls and rooflines clad in rusty weathering steel, but they need their build to blend in in a rural and unassuming seaside community

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Spanish architect Jaime and his wife Mimi as they embark on a mission to convert a Grade II listed folly into a family home in Buckinghamshire. Giving themselves a wildly optimistic six-month deadline to finish before the birth of their second child, it's not long before the couple hit trouble. Jaime puts all his energies and creativity into the project, using innovative 3D mapping to squeeze living spaces into the tower, but it proves to be an exhausting struggle

programme.title: Little British Isles with Alison Steadman
programme.desc: The actress Alison Steadman celebrates the natural beauty of some of the 6,000 islands that surround mainland Britain. Inspired by memories of a childhood holiday on the Isle of Man, Alison sets out to meet some of the people who live on the UK's 267 inhabited islands. She begins on Scotland's windswept Inner Hebrides, exploring the sparsely populated Gigha, Jura, Colonsay and Oronsay over the course of a week. She learns how to extract orchid essence, visits a fish farm, meets the keeper in charge of the country's only native honey bee reserve, and meets the resourceful natives thriving off the tough land that sustains them

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Headteacher Emma arrives in resus having been in a collision with a bus while cycling home from the school she works at. As she is treated, her husband Mick reflects on being informed what had happened. Meanwhile, 33-year-old Josh is treated for severe back pain, and 87-year-old Pamela is examined for a possible blood clot in her leg, and tells the doctors about her childhood in London during the Blitz

programme.title: Sex Party Secrets
programme.desc: As the popularity of exclusive arranged orgies rises, this documentary explores the hidden world of the UK's self-proclaimed 'sexual elite', providing an insight into the lives of the most selective party planners and their meticulously vetted clients. As the guests sip champagne and fulfil their wildest fantasies behind the doors of multimillion-pound mansions and country estates, the programme explores the motivation behind the desire that drives married couples, young single friends and seasoned sex addicts to seek fleeting intimate experiences and group encounters with strangers

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with Sean Lock and Stephen Mangan teaming up against Noel Fielding and Fay Ripley. The Brett Domino Trio are in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Headteacher Emma arrives in resus having been in a collision with a bus while cycling home from the school she works at. As she is treated, her husband Mick reflects on being informed what had happened. Meanwhile, 33-year-old Josh is treated for severe back pain, and 87-year-old Pamela is examined for a possible blood clot in her leg, and tells the doctors about her childhood in London during the Blitz

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick treats a border collie in a lot of pain with a suspicious lump on its ankle. However, the dog's recovery does not go to plan and proves extremely challenging. A short-haired cat arrives with a sore hip from jumping off the sofa, and it seems a hip replacement may be the best option, and a golden labrador is rushed into Fitzpatrick's sister clinic in Guildford with a lump growing on its side

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt investigates concerns about levels of naturally occurring arsenic in baby rice products, Jimmy Doherty examines Marks & Spencer's claim that they can trace all their beefburgers back to the cow, and Kate Quilton find out whether a parasite could be hiding in sushi. Programme exploring the secrets behind mass-produced food, meeting technicians, scientists, factory owners, growers and producers to find out exactly what goes into the groceries that end up on the supermarket shelves

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: After a long-term relationship came to an end and her son flew the nest, Alix Sinclare sold her family home in Devon. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Phil Kiernan invites the contestants to the Farmer's Boy Inn in Longhope, Gloucestershire, where he challenges them to a pie-making competition. However, the task proves less entertaining than the bickering that occurs between two guests as they try to share a kitchen

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Jane Asher host on the Geanna, their purpose-built hotel boat. Dan and Mardi Leonard are concerned space is too tight, while Sarah Langford and Jean-Pierre Kujawa worry about their privacy being invaded. A trip up the Thames allows the guests to sip champagne and get to know the ropes, but the mood at dinner is subdued, and when the owners cook their first breakfast on board they find themselves bickering

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sarah Langford and Jean-Pierre Kujawa, who have run the Sentry Mead B&B on the Isle of Wight for six years, play host in the last challenge of the week. The guests arrive with high expectations and after a few setbacks, the dinner and breakfast win everyone over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Tortoises Burt and Terrance become rivals in love when they pursue a breeding female. Meanwhile, six-year-old okapi K'Tusha nears the end of her first pregnancy, and keepers hope that her baby will help to conserve this highly-endangered species, and Asian elephant bull Aung-Bo senses that the three-day window for mating is about to begin

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim battles to get a fence built for their marauding flock of 25 sheep, and Krys battles to balance the books. They race to get their chateau ready for their first paying guests as Lee struggles with a chandelier. Elaine and David continue the search for their dream property, visiting a 17th-century ex-priory in Normandy and taking on board all that Dick has taught them

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Chris Moller meets a family who are building an experimental home on Wellington's Kapiti Coast, raised on stilts that are only metres from the high tide mark at Pukerua Bay

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Harry and Briony Anscombe, who have decided to move from London to Cornwall with their three young children and begin building work on an ambitious house, inspired by American modernist properties. With a budget of just £400,000, Harry opts to project manage himself, paying local builders by the hour, but his lack of experience in construction proves to be problematic

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: Albert and Kristina have moved into a coach house in Leamington Spa which was last decorated in the 1980s with a mock medieval style. With a baby on the way, the couple are desperate to make their new home into something special - so George kicks off by knocking a huge hole in the ceiling for extra headspace. Will his other ideas prove just as exciting? The architect also visits a 200-year-old farmhouse on the edge of the Peak District, where he faces difficulties as the owners are reluctant to let him knock down any walls

programme.title: World's Weirdest Homes
programme.desc: Part two of two. Charlie Luxton visits a Mexican house floating on 160,000 plastic bottles, a miniature cottage in Scotland, and a kaleidoscopic Canadian dwelling. He meets owners with an unusual attitude to their living space, including a Russian clown who likes to row his bed down a river, a carpenter who keeps building houses that get washed away by hurricanes, and a Cuban artist starting a revolution in his own neighbourhood

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 79-year-old man arrives in hospital after collapsing in the street whilst walking his dog, and doctors give him medication to try and stabilise his very high heart rate. Meanwhile, a young boy is treated after pushing a plastic toy down his ear, and doctors show concern for a man who has numbness in his arm and little finger, and send him for a CT scan

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with Jon Richardson and Phil Wang taking on guest captain Russell Howard and Roisin Conaty. Nick Helm joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: World's Weirdest Homes
programme.desc: Part two of two. Charlie Luxton visits a Mexican house floating on 160,000 plastic bottles, a miniature cottage in Scotland, and a kaleidoscopic Canadian dwelling. He meets owners with an unusual attitude to their living space, including a Russian clown who likes to row his bed down a river, a carpenter who keeps building houses that get washed away by hurricanes, and a Cuban artist starting a revolution in his own neighbourhood

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A two-year old mastiff with a bone tumour in its leg undergoes pioneering surgery, only to return a few weeks later suffering pain in its foot. A tiny pug faces the prospect of euthanasia after losing control of its hind legs, but neurologist Colin Driver thinks spinal surgery could give hope, and a Rhodesian ridgeback is brought in suffering from lameness. Surgery seems like the only option, but Noel and the owners are concerned whether this is the right course of action in the long run

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Sri Lanka to reveal the extraordinary lengths to which manufacturers go in order to split red lentils, and asks why they bother with this process. Kate Quilton learns why the way tea is drunk can affect its taste, and Matt Tebbutt finds out the difference between bubblegum and chewing gum

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Amber and Colin turn their bedroom into a dream vintage haven. After an initial assessment, the presenter begins by getting rid of any mass-produced furniture

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Recently retired couple Nick and Jane Purser are looking forward to settling down, but cannot decide where that should be. Jane's mother lives in Liverpool, so she would prefer to stay close by with a move to Lancaster. Nick wants them to move to southern France, where he can ride his motorbike in the sun. Jasmine Harman shows the pair three properties in Lancaster for up to £180,000 and Jonnie Irwin tempts them with three properties for up to £145,000 in France

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole restores a petrol pump and turns a hubcap into a clock, and Simon O'Brien repurposes a copper immersion heater into a stylish wash basin

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a Yorkshire textile mill, where Simon repurposes a mill trolley into a coffee table and Henry struggles to repair a Soviet-era motorbike

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim battles to get a fence built for their marauding flock of 25 sheep, and Krys battles to balance the books. They race to get their chateau ready for their first paying guests as Lee struggles with a chandelier. Elaine and David continue the search for their dream property, visiting a 17th-century ex-priory in Normandy and taking on board all that Dick has taught them

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton visits West Yorkshire, where Emma and Martin are battling wind and snow while building a stone home on an imposing hilltop. They've self-built before and Martin works in the trade, but the sheer scale of the project and an escalating budget leave the couple scrambling to finish before their family face homelessness

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Sylvie welcome their guests to the Watersedge Guest House in Barton-on-Sea, Hampshire, but an afternoon of deer-watching leaves some of them bored. Desperate to impress their fellow B&B owners, the pair host a Hawaiian barbecue, before pulling out all the stops at breakfast time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Owners Tim and Amelia Whitaker welcome their guests to their non-star-rated boutique accommodation, Nearwater, on the south Cornish coast, and take them out on a boat for a spot of seal watching and diving. However, the sight of wet suits proves too much for one contestant, and tensions also flare over dinner when Mike and Danny are quizzed about their relationship

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eva Buschmann hosts the competition at the Roundhouse Hotel in Weymouth, Dorset. She makes the mistake of assuming Danny Robinson and Mike Gibson are a couple, and offers them a double room with an exposed shower, but hopes a lesson in abstract art and a meal at her favourite harbour-side restaurant will win the other contestants over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike Gibson and Danny Robinson host their rival B&B owners at their four-star guesthouse - Homecliffe Hotel in Blackpool. The hosts take their visitors on a tram ride along the seafront and on to Madame Tussauds, but there are complaints about uncomfortable mattresses and disappointing views and the next day's breakfast fails to impress

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and air their grievances. Watersedge hosts Alan and Sylvie start the proceedings, but confront the guests who complained about their room having no en-suite facilities, before the winner is announced

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Visayan warty pig Wendy is fed up with the attentions of male pig Jake and decides to escape. Meanwhile, young giraffe Narus has been battling with other young males in the herd, so the keepers think that it's time to move him to a new zoo. Which is easier said than done

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Belinda travels to Dick and Angel's chateau so that Angel can teach her the art of decoupage on a classic 1950s family dressing table. It's changeover day at Krys and Tim's chateau, as they sweat it out in 42-degree heat to get their 20-room chateau ready for their next booking. Budding chateau owners Elaine and David visit a 19th-century chateau with 18 rooms and two gîtes in Normandy

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: The inner-city Auckland suburb of Ponsonby is full of pretty, historic, wooden villas, and the locals are very protective of them. So when Francophiles Sarah and Philippe Lods decided to build a modernist home, using industrial materials such as concrete and steel, on a rare empty site there, they faced some challenges. Chris Moller traces their progress

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Gallery owner Elinor and fitness entrepreneur Born set themselves the task of creating Britain's first hypoallergenic house in a leafy back garden plot in south-west London. Driven by the need to alleviate their two young sons' life-threatening allergies, their new part-submerged, shed-inspired home, will be constructed using low toxin materials and deploy cutting-edge filtration systems to clean the air they breathe. Will their innovative new house deliver the benefits Elinor and Born are hoping for?

programme.title: The Same Sky
programme.desc: Ralf tries ineffectually to prevent Lars falling prey to his feelings for Sabine, while Conrad must choose between saving his conscience or his family as he can't save both. And Axel and the other diggers learn that the door to freedom is rarely open. Detective thriller, starring Tom Schilling, Sofia Helin and Friederike Becht. In German and English

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 64-year-old man is brought to St George's in an induced coma, having been thrown from his bicycle at high speed, with his wife by his side for support. Drama student Florian, 21, tripped and hurt his foot rushing to class, and he explains how he and his family fled war in Kosovo when he was a child, and the sacrifices his father made to get his family to safety

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Stuart, who's 40, is admitted to hospital after being involved in a high-speed collision with multiple cyclists during a race, and a CT scan reveals that he is bleeding internally. Meanwhile, 83-year-old Elicia-Jean collapsed at home after experiencing dizziness, and doctors think she may have an infection

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of Lela's, a California restaurant that is currently being run into the ground by its inexperienced owner. Gordon tries to turn the failing business around, but faces opposition from the chef and makes enemies when he exposes the suspect behaviour of some members of staff

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 64-year-old man is brought to St George's in an induced coma, having been thrown from his bicycle at high speed, with his wife by his side for support. Drama student Florian, 21, tripped and hurt his foot rushing to class, and he explains how he and his family fled war in Kosovo when he was a child, and the sacrifices his father made to get his family to safety

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a six-year-old shih-tzu that can barely walk due to the severe arthritis in its hip, as well as a two-year-old Labrador suffering osteoarthritis in all four legs. He also helps a four-month-old German shepherd with a genetic disease affecting its joints - but the test results are worse than the professor feared and It will require all his surgical skills to repair

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty is in the US state of Maryland to find out why soft-shell crab is such a delicacy, Kate Quilton asks what in nature provides the bright blue dye used in sweets and Matt Tebbutt reveals why there is jelly in pork pies and the lengths scientists go to in order to put it there

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank find a huge sculpture of Elvis and trawl through a trailer full of rare art and military memorabilia

programme.title: Celebrity Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas, Nancy Dell'Olio, Tim Vine and Chris Kamara fight for the lots at auction

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Frank and Danielle visit a former trading post and a hospital that houses a collection of medical paraphernalia

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike bids for a 1940s motorcycle, while Danielle gets excited over a gun appraisal

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: John Heussi and his team work on a high-performance BMW 330, the Interceptor, while a shortage of staff sees Dave manning the phones

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: In an edition first broadcast in 2007, Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May celebrate British Leyland's 40th birthday by trying to boost the reputation of the cars that once rolled off its production line. The Aston Martin DBS is put through its paces on the test track, Jennifer Saunders is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car and the team asks whatever happened to the beach buggy

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motor racing driver Lewis Hamilton demonstrates his abilities on the test track, and Richard Hammond tries his hand in a Formula One car. Jeremy Clarkson and James May assess historic vehicles to establish which one pioneered the control layout used in modern cars, and singer-songwriter James Blunt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Far Eastern Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef ends his exploration of the Far East's most diverse food cultures by visiting Bangladesh, sampling the curry on offer in the country that has played a major role in establishing the success of Indian restaurants throughout the UK

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team takes the BMW M3, Mercedes-Benz C63 AMG and Audi RS4 to Spain for a thorough test of performance. David Tennant is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, James May drives a Jaguar XF and Richard Hammond wonders what happened to that ultimate symbol of 1960s grooviness - the beach buggy

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May compete in a 1950s-style race from London to Edinburgh. On the footplate of the A1 Tornado steam train, Jeremy has to feed the boiler with a constant supply of coal, while Richard braves the saddle of a Vincent Black Shadow motorbike and James drives an old Jaguar XK120 as the trio discover what Top Gear might have been like 60 years ago. Michael Schumacher drives the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade and comedian Joe Lycett head to the mini break mecca of Amsterdam. Their two-day hurtle through the city's attractions takes in culture, canals, cuisine, and other things not necessarily beginning with C. As part of their whistle-stop tour, the duo doss down in one of the city's hippest hotels, take a battery-powered boat tour, do some drawing, and peer into the world of the water bear. The brave travellers also sample some Dutch gin known as jenever, and visit the city's street organ museum

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade and actress Sally Phillips head to the Swedish capital for a fulsome two days of fun. Despite having no head for heights, the duo fearlessly sample the city's only rooftop tour, before wrestling with the concept of fika - coffee and cake. The pair also a visit to the museum established in homage to 1970s pop group Abba and attend an interactive late-night art show involving their phones. After all this, there's still time to sample some meatballs, ride on a pedalo and experience the world's first ice bar

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ellie Taylor, Steve Pemberton, Fern Britton and Josh Widdicombe test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by actors Kevin Bishop and Brian Blessed, comedian Josh Widdicombe and historian Kate Williams, who aim to help the team captains deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by comedy actors Warwick Davis and Hugh Dennis, comedian Katherine Ryan and journalist John Simpson, who aim to help the team captains deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: In an episode first broadcast in 2013, Mrs Brown's Boys star Brendan O'Carroll and stand-ups Jo Brand and Phill Jupitus join regular panellist Alan Davies for a festive edition of the comedy quiz. Host Stephen Fry asks them a range of unusual questions on the theme of Kris Kringle and other Christmas-related topics

programme.title: Meet the Richardsons
programme.desc: Chris Kamara asks Jon to take part in a Leeds United fundraiser for deprived children. While he's away Lucy tries to enrol Elsie in a private school - against Jon's wishes

programme.desc: Dara O'Briain, Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons are joined by Josh Widdicombe, Katherine Ryan, Milton Jones and Ed Gamble to give their irreverent takes on the biggest stories to hit the headlines, through stand-up spots and improvised games. First aired in September 2015

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Taskmaster

programme.title: Rick Stein's Mediterranean Escapes
programme.desc: The chef samples Mediterranean food and visits his favourite places in the region. In the first programme he catches a ferry to the mountainous island of Corsica, where he tastes sausages and sheep's cheese, and discovers the locals have a passion for chestnuts

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond, Jeremy Clarkson and James May try to find the perfect car for 17-year-olds, dealing with insurance companies, accident repairs and a muddy music festival car park in the process. Richard also visits Abu Dhabi to test the Lamborghini Murcielago SV and stages a race between the 1000hp Bugatti Veyron and the McLaren F1. Actor, writer and comedian Stephen Fry is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters find three small cars at an affordable price - a task that involves pestering bankers, harnessing large dogs and almost getting arrested. James May meets American rally driver Ken Block, and Jeremy Clarkson tests out the Mercedes SL Black. Comedian Michael McIntyre is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade returns with the offbeat tourism show and is joined in the first edition by comedian and Not Going Out star Lee Mack for a short break in the Belgian capital. Their two-day trip features culture, comics, pralines, puppets and attractions both large and small, including the Grand Place and the famous atom-shaped Atomium, as well as waffles, mussels - including Moules Ghenghis Khan - and the secrets of chocolate-making

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by Cold Feet star Fay Ripley for a wintry mini-break in Norway's capital, where they first head to the Thief hotel - home to an art collection including works by Warhol, Hirst, and Koons, as well as a Bryan Ferry-themed dining room. They also take a ride on the city's most popular toboggan run and try to master cross-country skiing, before visiting the National Gallery to check out Edvard Munch's masterpiece The Scream, and sampling Michelin-starred food at Kontrast

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. Sue Perkins, Reginald D Hunter and Jimmy Carr join regular panellist Alan Davies on the comedy quiz. Host Stephen Fry asks a range of fiendish questions on the topic of kitsch, with points being awarded for interesting answers as well as correct ones

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites satirist Charlie Brooker, presenter Scarlett Moffatt and actress Pearl Mackie to express their anger over individuals, events and objects that upset them, hoping to curry the host's favour and see them consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include crocodiles, mosquitoes and having one's hair cut

programme.title: Rick Stein's Mediterranean Escapes
programme.desc: The chef continues to explore his favourite locations in the Mediterranean, visiting a lagoon in Sardinia where he watches fishermen catching mullet using ancient Roman methods. He also boards a ferry heading for the city of Palermo in Sicily and discovers one of the most impressive pasta dishes of his career

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond and James May test the Porsche Panamera against the Royal Mail's delivery service, racing a first-class letter to see which travels faster over the full length of the British Isles. Jeremy Clarkson gets chased by the British Army in some of their most deadly toys, and multi Olympic gold medal-winning sprinter Usain Bolt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May each buy a rear-wheel-drive coupe for £1,500 and find themselves entered into a French ice race. Richard discovers a way to fill the waiting time for flights by inventing the sport of airport-vehicle racing, Jeremy gets behind the wheel of the BMW Z4, and actress Sienna Miller is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade and Robert Webb explore the Portuguese island, including sampling the scary-looking black scabbard fish with banana at a remote seafront restaurant that can be reached only by sea or cable car. They also head to Funchal for an embroidery lesson and muse over the merits of a Madeiran mini-break while enjoying afternoon tea on the terrace

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by comedian Shazia Mirza for a two-day break in the French Riviera, hiring a three-wheeled car for a scenic tour of Nice as well as checking out Cap Ferrat, blue art, fancy yachts and a grotto in Monaco. Later, they take a pricey helicopter ride before they end with a trip to the casino - hoping to win back the budget overspend on the helicopter

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedians Ellie Taylor and Josh Widdicombe, actor and writer Steve Pemberton and presenter Fern Britton take part in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell feature in a special episode of out-takes, looking back over some of the best moments. Among the celebrity guests are Kevin Bishop, Warwick Davis, Romesh Ranganathan, Hugh Dennis, Sara Cox and Brian Blessed

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by space scientist Maggie Aderin-Pocock, comedian Phil Wang, actor Jason Watkins and EastEnders star Adam Woodyatt, aiming to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition of the comedy quiz, with actress and TV and radio presenter Liza Tarbuck, comedienne Susan Calman and broadcaster Sandi Toksvig joining regular panellist Alan Davies. Host Stephen Fry asks a range of questions on a kaleidoscope of topics beginning with 'k', with points being awarded for interesting answers as well as correct ones

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Topical comedy show, with Ellie Taylor, Josh Widdicombe, Rob Beckett and James Acaster joining regulars Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons to give their irreverent takes on the biggest stories to hit the headlines hit the headlines in October 2015, through stand-up spots and improvised games. Chaired by Dara O Briain

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Topical comedy show, with Rob Beckett, Ed Byrne, Milton Jones, Romesh Ranganathan and Holly Walsh joining Dara O Briain and Hugh Dennis to take a comic look at recent world events, through stand-up and improvised games. First shown in October 2015

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Russell Howard uses ear wax to charm a nice lady called Carol, Asim Chaudhry goes digging for invertebrates and Liza Tarbuck makes a puzzle for children

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Gary Lineker hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with panellists Ross Noble and Samira Ahmed joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell feature in a special episode of out-takes, looking back over some of the best moments. Among the celebrity guests are Kevin Bishop, Warwick Davis, Romesh Ranganathan, Hugh Dennis, Sara Cox and Brian Blessed

Host Stephen Fry asks a range of questions on a kaleidoscope of topics beginning with 'k', with points being awarded for interesting answers as well as correct ones

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: It's time for the grand final, which features peas, teas and absent keys, as Ed Gamble, Jo Brand, Katy Wix and Rose Matafeo vie to go home with Greg Davies's massive golden face

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Monster Ships
programme.desc: A profile of the Royal Clipper, officially the largest square-rigged ship in the world, which operates as a glamorous high-end cruise ship for sailing enthusiasts

programme.title: Monster Ships
programme.desc: Specially designed to install wind turbines far out to sea, the Orca has the impressive ability to raise its hull a full 17 metres out of the water

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team help the Bamburgh Research Project uncover the Saxon roots of the local fortress

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The battle for North Africa, which involved three years of fighting between Rommel's Afrika Korps and Montgomery's Desert Rats and culminated in the Allied victory at El Alamein. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The story of the Allied forces hunt for Joachim Peiper, the man responsible for the massacre of American PoWs in Malmedy, Belgium

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A secretive underground base that concealed the power to destroy nations, and a sprawling industrial relic clinging to the edge of an unforgiving waterfall

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide to venture beyond Europe as he travels through the Holy Land. He begins in Israel, learning about the Baha'i faith and how it spread to Britain in the Edwardian era, hearing the story of the origins of Tel Aviv, and visiting the Christian, Jewish and Muslim quarters of the capital Jerusalem. Michael then crosses the separation barrier between Jerusalem and the West Bank in the company of his Palestinian guide and meets the embroiderers of the Arab Women's Union in Bethlehem, before finishing his journey in the Negev desert

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Scone Palace in Perthshire, which they first visited back in March, discovering several items with historical connections. They include a 19th-century military campaign box said to have been stolen from Napoleon, a portrait of a woman who helped save the lives of hundreds of Jews in occupied Holland during the Second World War and a silver tea service that was once to be melted down to raise funds for a Spitfire. A teacher also brings in a leather strap, a fearsome implement of corporal punishment that wasn't outlawed until the 1980s

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: A major service disruption hits Paddington on Meet the Manager day, and there is a look at Brunel's last engineering masterpiece, the Royal Albert Bridge, which spans the River Tamar and connects Devon and Cornwall. A fitter has to repair a faulty peak-time train full of passengers, and Charlbury station prepares for the Wilderness festival

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: New York Architect Wendy Fok travels to the remote Guizhou Valley in China to see the Beipanjiang First Bridge, the highest bridge on Earth, which boasts a 20,000 tonne steel deck. She soon discovers that trying to straddle a 565 metre gorge so deep that it's known as the 'crack in the Earth', presented huge challenges for its engineers. Plus, Andrew Steele tracks down a bridge on the thames that nobody thought could ever be built, Suzie Sheehy is in Hampshire climbing the tallest unreinforced concrete town ever built and Luke Bisby scales the Forth Bridge in Edinburgh

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A marooned ironclad structure off the coast of England that hides a dramatic story of war and resilience, and a facility in the heart of America that helped tear apart a nation

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The pursuit of former Austrian policeman Franz Stangl, who served as commandant of the Nazi extermination camps Sobibor and Treblinka

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the myth of German invincibility was finally debunked by their defeat at the Battle of Stalingrad. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo follows in the footsteps of Edwardian travellers to trace a route recommended in his Bradshaw's guide from the heart of France to the Mediterranean coast. His journey begins in Lyon, where he learns how the city got its gastronomic reputation, and takes instruction from a leading chef on making an omelette. In Avignon, he samples a glass of the region's famous wine Chateauneuf-du-Pape, before his journey ends at the Mediterranean port of Marseille, where he joins a pilot boat as it leads a supertanker to its berth

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: The Vatican is home to classified files that could reveal top-secret ties to the Nazis, the Mafia, and extraterrestrials

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright attends a former customer's funeral, leaving Granville in charge of the store. However, the miserly shopkeeper has difficulty trusting his nephew to run it properly

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Miserly shopkeeper Arkwright organises a sales drive in a bid to boost profits - and rid himself of the vast consignment of apples he has mistakenly ordered. An unhappy Granville is persuaded to demonstrate the merchandise. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the Roundhouse, Derby, a vast former railway shed where dozens of trains were once serviced. Items brought in by the public include a pair of Clarice Cliff bookends won in a darts tournament, a silver christening cup with a surprising history and a picture by a renowned British artist, painted on a fragment of a pillowcase in a PoW camp. The Uncovered feature sees the return of a silver claret jug awarded to Hallam football club in 1867, with recent research prompting specialist Alastair Dickenson to rethink its value

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the work of the train operating company's staff along the route between London, south-west England and south Wales. The first edition focuses on problems caused by last winter's storms, with the worst weather for a century battering the nation and pushing employees and passengers to the limit. Network Rail's Steve Hawkins and his engineers have their work cut out after the line at Dawlish in Devon is washed away, while Dave Slater and his team at Network Control have to battle the elements to keep trains running as storm after storm floods the network

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the incredible machines built to get troops directly to the front line, on land, at sea and in the air

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Forgotten relics from the defence of America's homeland, concealed by the banks of the Mississippi, and a concrete behemoth on the French coast that proved invulnerable to attacks

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The story of German architect Albert Speer, one of the only war criminals to accept his guilt during the Nuremberg trials, although there were doubts about the scale of his remorse

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How German U-boats were used to disrupt the flow of goods from America across the Atlantic Ocean in an attempt to starve Britain into submission

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his trusty 1913 Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide, Michael Portillo travels east through the Balkans along the most exotic section of the route taken by the Orient Express. His journey begins in Sofia, capital of Bulgaria, and includes stops in the ancient city of Plovdiv, the region of Rumelia, and former capital of the Ottoman Empire Edirne. Arriving in Istanbul, Michael takes a trip on the Bosphorus - a much-coveted stretch of water - finds out about Turkish delight and travels along the Marmaray metro line, which connects European and Asian Istanbul via a tunnel beneath the Bosphorus

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Within a dense Polish woodland sits a forgotten complex with an explosive history. Also, grand coastal defences in Ghana reveal a tortured past

programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates the story of the D-Day landings in June 1944, when the Allies launched the biggest ever amphibious invasion on the beaches of Normandy. Although both sides suffered heavy casualties, it proved to be the turning point in the war against Germany

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Chenies Manor, near Amersham in Buckinghamshire, where members of the public bring along treasured antiques and collectibles to hear their stories and discover their worth. Items include a chair designed by a family better known for luxury cars, a painting by one of Britain's first modern artists and a helmet brought in by a collector of Star Wars memorabilia. Specialist Freya Yuan also translates a poem engraved on a bamboo pot, and there's a debate on antique ivory with Will Travers of the Born Free Foundation and Marjorie Trusted of the V&A Museum

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: As rugby union's Six Nations Championship kicks off in Cardiff, train manager Jodie Edwards and her team try to get thousands of fans home after the match. Penzance in Cornwall remains shut off from the rest of the network as engineers work around the clock to get services running through Dawlish, where the line was washed away, while new duty manager Joe has his hands full on his first Saturday night shift at London's Paddington station when the last Wales-bound train of the evening has a problem

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The Independent, a skyscraper in Austin, Texas. At a height of 690ft, it is the tallest building in the city, surpassing the 683ft Austonian, another residential skyscraper

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An American prison for the violent and deranged with links to an infamous cult leader, and a cavernous tunnel network in France offering shelter from unrelenting bombardment

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The search for Josef Mengele, one of the most wanted Nazi war criminals, which was led by Holocaust survivor Simon Wiesenthal

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Account of Operation Barbarossa, Germany's plan to invade the Soviet Union in 1941, revealing the massive human and financial cost endured by the Soviet people, who fought a bloody and lonely war with little support from the Allies. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Vienna, Michael Portillo encounters a pre-Cold War spy and learns about the concert that caused a riot in 1913. He then travels the Habsburg imperial line across the Semmering Pass, a line blasted through the Alps. In Graz, the former politician ventures underground at the Lurgrotte Caves to find out about a famous rescue operation of the past, then in Slovenia discovers how an earthquake in Ljubljana prompted its citizens to assert their national identity in architecture and art. Arriving in the Italian port of Trieste, Michael savours the imported coffee that fuelled a cafe culture

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Broughton Castle, near Banbury, Oxfordshire. Among the finds brought in to be valued are an art nouveau vase, a Georgian dolls' house, a Victorian baby bouncer, a letter from Lord Nelson, a book of early police mug shots, a collection of talismans and a musical penknife. Plus, Philip Mould meets a tax inspector who was paid in paintings by several artists

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek auctions off some vehicles that were used in the TV show Heartbeat. There's love for an Austin Cambridge and a sporty Italian number finds a new home

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: Examining the complex system the Nazis set up to implement the extermination of the Jewish race throughout Europe from 1942, with Auschwitz as the hub. The programme reveals that at other camps, including Treblinka, Belzec and Sobibor, rival commanders participated in the genocide with enthusiasm and shared ideas for the best method of mass murder

programme.title: Chasing Classic Cars
programme.desc: Car specialist Evan Ide discovers an untouched, 1966 Shelby GT 350 Carry Over Car that has been stashed in storage for more than 40 years

programme.title: Chasing Classic Cars

programme.desc: The Karmann Ghia looks beautiful, but it never went as fast as it looks. Moggy and his team have been asked to convert it to electric power

programme.desc: A look at how thousands of tonnes of grapefruit are picked and processed, and how conveyor belts that carry millions of pieces of luggage every year are built

programme.desc: The group is excited when a legendary 1991 Toyota MR2 Turbo is found. However, one small fix becomes a big problem for Ant as the entire engine has to be removed

programme.title: Vintage Voltage
programme.desc: A mother and son enlist the help of Moggy to revamp their Fiat 500. The team members need to be creative to find space for electric components in this tiny city car

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: The processes involved in turning scrap metal into luxury cookers. Plus, the hand-rolling of billions of sticks of incense, and the creation of jenever - the original form of gin

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: Examining how some farms are run with the help of robots, how a machine picks up 30 tons of logs in a single grab, and how the world's most valuable pianos are transported

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Swedish-American Judi Lembke hosts the first dinner party in London, hoping her relaxed attitude and an aperitif of schnapps will put the guests at ease and help her win the £1,000 cash prize. In an attempt to keep stress to a minimum, she prepares all the dishes in advance and plans to pass off shop-bought pastry as her own, unaware that some of her diners are self-proclaimed food snobs

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Houseproud legal adviser Lisa Connell throws the second party in London, but her perfectly planned dinner is thrown into disarray by some tricky Parmesan crisps and a temperamental oven. The kitchen crisis quickly becomes the least of Lisa's worries when fellow diner Judi Lembke spills a glass of red wine on her cream couch

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teddy bear-loving deputy headmaster Peter Rixham hosts the third dinner party in London, hoping to make his guests feel at home with special place settings, kitsch music and a stuffed deer's head. Things start to go awry, however, when Peter cannot find the key to his shed, where the dining chairs are stored

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Moni Bharij prepares a lavish Indian banquet for the fourth dinner party in London, cooking a starter of king prawns, followed by five main courses and a strawberry and cream cake topped with glitter for dessert. However, after the host insisted on drinking her favourite wine at the other contestants' houses, the tables are turned when Lisa Connell decides to do the same

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Matthew Baker banishes his parents from the house and prepares a menu involving fruit soup and solo tortellono in basil leaf for the final meal in London. The music-lover, who models himself on Jamie Oliver, hopes to impress his guests and win the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman shows five properties to Pat and David, who have been married for 44 years and are selling up in Northampton to move to Portugal's Silver Coast

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Retired couple Sue and Dave Atkins from Norwich have spent their time travelling since finishing work, and have now decided the Tampa Bay area in Florida is the best place to escape the cold British weather. With a budget of just over £100,000, Sara Damergi shows them five ideal properties

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Plus, Karen and Paul aim to transform the old stable and coach house into a space fit for hosting weddings

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part one of two. Robert and Amy prepare to tie the knot in a classic Barone family wedding ceremony - but the groom becomes suspicious when his future brother-in-law Peter tries to make peace with both him and Ray. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Brad Garrett

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part two of two. Robert and Amy finally get married in the Barone family tradition. Brad Garrett and Monica Horan star

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of Mangia Mangia, an Italian restaurant with an identity crisis in Woodland Park, Colorado, where guests have a choice between casual fine-dining service or a pick-up from the drive-through takeaway. Hard-nosed owner Julie finds herself completely at odds with the head chef, forcing her to breaking point

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sushi Ko, a Japanese restaurant in California that is losing up to $20,000 a month. Gordon finds owner and chef Akira lacking in confidence and self-esteem as the strain of an empty business is beginning to take its toll on his relationship with this family

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas tries to find the perfect place for Tony and Carol, who have been married for 40 years and are looking for their dream holiday home in the Spanish town of Sucina

programme.desc: Chris and Kay from Buckinghamshire are searching for their perfect retirement home to share with their two cats. The only hitch is that they can't agree on where it should be. Kay loves the stunning scenery of Cardigan Bay in west Wales, while sun-worshipper Chris would rather move to the beautiful Mar Menor in Spain. Jasmine Harman selects three properties for their £150,000 budget in Wales, but Jonnie Irwin has three cracking options in the Mar Menor

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Matt Lucas host the grand final of this year's contest. The last three bakers face three tests of a range of baking skills - a deceptively simple Signature round combining set custard and puff pastry, a Technical celebrating the classic combination of chocolate and nuts, and a magnificent dessert tower for their final Showstopper, before judges Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood select this year's winner

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The second half of the comedy challenge series kicks off here, and it looks as if four of the contestants still have a good chance of lifting Taskmaster Greg Davies's golden head in the final - regular viewers will no doubt have guessed whose chances are looking rather more bleak. This week, Daisy May Cooper gets rather cross, Mawaan Rizwan learns a lesson about waterfowl and Richard Herring wrings out a wet sock

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes two more singletons to choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. Six-foot pansexual opera singer Clare is looking for a man as tall as she is, while Jessica, who has a disastrous dating history, hopes to bring out her naughty side

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Look backing at some of the most memorable stories filmed at King's College Hospital, including a young woman who had been knocked unconscious in an attack on her way home. Elsewhere, 91-year-old Rose is rushed to King's with a suspected stroke, and reminisces about the strength and resilience of her own grandmother, and a junior doctor talks about his background growing up in Liverpool

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A look back at the showbiz singletons who bravely embarked on blind dates with unsuspecting members of the public, including Alexandra Burke's date with professional model Louis, and Esther Rantzen's rendezvous with Irish defence lawyer John. The programme also looks back at the dates of DJ Roman Kemp, Conservative MP Michael Fabricant and former Gogglebox star Sandra Martin

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: With autumn just around the corner, it's a chance for the Strawbridge family to make the most of the wild larder on their doorstep. Dick is determined to make sloe gin, while Angel presses on with her plan to create a library in the pepper pot tower

programme.title: The Truth About Amazon
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Sabrina Grant investigate the online retailer, offering consumer advice and exposing questionable practices with insight from executives, tech analysts and experts. The first episode takes a look at how to get the best deals possible, reports on review hijacking, and reveals how the company pushes customers towards products offering poor value for money

programme.title: Kirstie's Christmas: Quick & Easy Craft
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp creates quick and easy crafts for the festive season. She is joined by Phil Spencer, who gets the party started with some cocktail magic. Kirstie's sister, florist Sofie, helps with some festive florals that can be scaled up or down for any size of gathering. And in the kitchen, baker Nancy Birtwhistle has a raft of recipes, tips and tricks for all things foodie and festive

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Charity worker Jane Longhurst is joined at her home by a radio DJ, an engineer, a competitive would-be chef and a barmaid for the first dinner party in Bradford. She struggles to find halal chicken, and burns part of her dessert when she gets distracted by flirting among her guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the second party in Bradford, radio DJ Khaliver Ashrafi prepares traditional dishes from her Pakistani heritage. The menu proves too much for self-confessed 'spice wimp' Jane Longhurst, but draws praise from competitive foodie Stuart Hudson, who considers himself a curry connoisseur

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Engineer Nigel Beaumont hosts the third dinner party in Bradford, hoping to impress his guests with a seafood platter, pork and a millefeuille. However, the choice of wildflowers as table decorations annoys some of the diners, and the main course fails to hit the mark when he allows the food to go cold

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: With tempers among the diners still frayed after the previous night's party, barmaid Theresa Walsh hopes an Italian-themed menu will lighten the mood on the penultimate evening in Bradford. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
